basic_info.name: Sanya Atlantis Aquaventure Waterpark Wave Pool (三亚·亚特兰蒂斯水世界浪池)
basic_info.location: Atlantis Sanya Resort, Haitang Bay National Coast, Sanya, Hainan Province, China
basic_info.operator: Atlantis Sanya (operated by Kerzner International on behalf of owner Fosun Tourism Group / Fosun International)

technical_specs.wave_technology_brand: Murphy's Waves (wave generation system); pool designed by HAS of Texas [uncertain]
technical_specs.pool_size: Approximately 7,000 m² wave pool (some sources cite 6,400 m²) [uncertain]
technical_specs.wave_height: Approximately 0.3-1.5 m, family-oriented recreational waves (not designed for stand-up surfing) [uncertain]
technical_specs.wave_height_adjustability: Yes - 7 different wave modes/patterns are programmable, ranging from calm pool conditions to larger swells; specific tier names not publicly published [uncertain]
technical_specs.wave_types: Recreational rolling waves and white-water surge waves for floating, bobbing, and bodyboarding; not shaped for surfing barrels or open-face rides
technical_specs.waves_per_hour: [uncertain] - operates in cycles with rest intervals; not publicly documented
technical_specs.wave_count_per_set: [uncertain] - cycles between active wave modes and calm intervals on a programmed schedule; specific set count not published
technical_specs.session_length: Continuous access during park operating hours (no per-session limits); wave cycles run intermittently throughout the day [uncertain]
technical_specs.stance_rotation_schedule: N/A - not a surfing-focused pool; no Goofy/Regular rotation
technical_specs.wave_type_classification: Artificial recreational wave pool (Murphy's Waves family-style), not a dedicated surf-shaped wave pool

pricing_sessions.price_range: Aquaventure Waterpark day ticket from approximately RMB 198-338 for adults (varies by season and platform); child ticket from RMB 258; included free for hotel guests of Atlantis Sanya. Wave pool access is included in the general waterpark admission
pricing_sessions.session_skill_tiers: N/A - no skill-tiered surf sessions; single general admission grants access to wave pool
pricing_sessions.equipment_rental: Life jackets provided free of charge; bodyboards/inflatable tubes available for rent or use within the park [uncertain on exact fees]
pricing_sessions.coaching_fee: N/A - no surf coaching offered at the wave pool; the pool is recreational only
pricing_sessions.coaching_packages: N/A - no surf coaching packages
pricing_sessions.membership_program: Atlantis Sanya offers annual passes and resort membership through Fosun's Club Med-affiliated programs; specific waterpark-only membership pricing varies seasonally [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.corporate_team_building: Yes - Atlantis Sanya offers MICE/team-building packages including waterpark buyouts and group event hosting via the resort's events department

experience_guide.best_season: Year-round operation (only year-round waterpark in China); peak experience April-October when air and water temperatures are warmest; avoid Chinese national holidays (Spring Festival, Golden Week, summer school holidays) for shorter queues
experience_guide.best_swell_window: N/A - artificial wave pool
experience_guide.crowd_level: Very high during Chinese public holidays and July-August summer school break; moderate on weekends; lower on weekdays in shoulder season (March, May, September, November). Wave pool can be heavily crowded during peak wave shows
experience_guide.booking_method: Online pre-purchase strongly recommended; tickets sold on official website, OTA platforms, and at park gate
experience_guide.booking_platform: Atlantis Sanya official website (atlantissanya.com), Ctrip/Trip.com, Fliggy, Meituan/Dianping, Klook, KKday, WeChat Mini Program
experience_guide.payment_options: Alipay, WeChat Pay, UnionPay, international credit cards (Visa/Mastercard/Amex), cash (RMB)
experience_guide.skill_required: Zero - no surfing skill required; suitable for non-swimmers in shallow areas with life jackets
experience_guide.age_eligibility: Children under 0.9 m height free; 0.9-1.22 m children's ticket; over 1.22 m adult ticket. Children under 13 require adult supervision. Some thrill rides have minimum 1.22 m height requirement; wave pool itself accessible to all ages with supervision
experience_guide.child_friendly_score: 5 - highly child-friendly; shallow zero-entry edges, life jackets provided free, adjacent Splashers children's play zone, lifeguards posted around the wave pool
experience_guide.sup_paddleboard_offering: Not offered within the wave pool [uncertain]
experience_guide.photography_video_service: On-site professional photographers and ride photo capture available at major slides; photo packages sold at park exit kiosks; pricing varies [uncertain on exact rates]
experience_guide.language_support: Yes - English, Mandarin, Cantonese, and Russian language support available at guest services; multilingual signage throughout the park
experience_guide.accessibility_for_disabled: Limited - wheelchair accessibility for general pathways and viewing areas; no dedicated adaptive surfing program; specific accessibility features for wave pool entry not publicly documented [uncertain]
experience_guide.stance_preference_input: N/A - not a surf training pool
experience_guide.safety_notes: Lifeguards posted at all pools per international waterpark safety standards; mandatory life jackets for non-swimmers and children; wave cycle warnings broadcast before each set; pregnant women, those with heart conditions, or recent surgery patients advised against the wave pool. Insurance included in ticket per Chinese theme park regulations
experience_guide.events_competitions_calendar: Hosts in-pool entertainment shows (DJ sets, live music, fire-dance, acrobatics on the central stage) seasonally; does not host competitive surfing events

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: Haitang Bay area hotels including Conrad Sanya, Park Hyatt Sanya, Shangri-La Sanya, Kempinski Haitang Bay, Mandarin Oriental Sanya - typical rates RMB 1,500-5,000+ per night
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: Atlantis Sanya Hotel - 1,314 rooms and suites including Underwater Suites with Ambassador Lagoon views; rates from approximately RMB 2,000 standard room to RMB 100,000+ for signature underwater suites; waterpark and aquarium access included for hotel guests
surroundings.nearby_food: Atlantis Sanya houses 21 in-house restaurants and bars (Ossiano underwater dining, Bread Street Kitchen by Gordon Ramsay, Seafire steakhouse, Ling Long Chinese, etc.); Haitang Bay Duty Free Shopping Mall (CDF Mall) nearby with extensive dining; Haitang Bay Shilipuxin food street
surroundings.transport: Self-drive (resort parking available), taxi/Didi, hotel shuttle from Sanya Phoenix International Airport (SYX), tour bus services from Sanya city
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: Approximately 35-40 km / 45-50 minutes drive from Sanya Phoenix International Airport (SYX); approximately 25 km from Sanya City center; close to Haitang Bay High-Speed Railway Station (5-10 minutes drive)
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Combine with Haitang Bay CDF Duty Free Mall shopping, Wuzhizhou Island day trip, Yalong Bay beaches, Sanya Bay sunset, Nanshan Buddhism Cultural Park; multi-day Atlantis stay packages bundle waterpark, aquarium, dolphin/sealion encounters, and underwater dining

pros: Year-round indoor/outdoor operation - China's only all-year waterpark
Massive 200,000 m² waterpark with 36 slides and major attractions beyond the wave pool
Wave pool features live entertainment stage with DJs, music, and performances
Free for Atlantis Sanya hotel guests with multi-day re-entry
Family-friendly with strong safety oversight, free life jackets, and supervised play zones
cons: Extremely crowded during Chinese national holidays and summer school break - long slide queues
Wave pool is recreational only - not suitable for stand-up surfing or skill development
Premium pricing compared to local Hainan waterparks
Locker, food, and photo upsells add significantly to total spend
Wave intensity is mild and family-oriented; advanced surfers will find it underwhelming
reviews_reputation.user_reviews: Dianping (大众点评) average rating approximately 4.5-4.7/5; Xiaohongshu (小红书) abundant positive coverage praising the visual scale, wave pool ambiance, and themed atmosphere; Mafengwo (马蜂窝) and Ctrip reviews highlight the wave pool's entertainment shows and family suitability while noting peak-season crowding [uncertain on exact current scores]
reviews_reputation.recommended_for: Families with children, leisure tourists, photo/social-media visitors, hotel guests at Atlantis Sanya, corporate group buyouts; NOT recommended for serious surfers seeking training waves
reviews_reputation.operator_brand_partnership: Atlantis brand owned by Kerzner International (UAE/Bahamas heritage); Atlantis Sanya is owned by Fosun Tourism Group (Fosun International), with Kerzner providing brand and management. Wave technology by Murphy's Waves (UK); slides by ProSlide Technology (Canada); waterpark designed by HAS of Texas (USA)

verification_sources: https://www.atlantissanya.com/marine-and-waterpark/aquaventure-waterpark - Atlantis Sanya official site Aquaventure Waterpark page
https://blooloop.com/water-parks/in-depth/atlantis-sanya-aquaventure-hainan-island/ - Blooloop in-depth feature confirming opening date, designers, Murphy's Waves wave system
https://us.trip.com/travel-guide/attraction/sanya/sanya-atlantis-aquaventure-waterpark-44775645/ - Trip.com booking and current operating info
https://www.travelchinaguide.com/attraction/hainan/atlantis-sanya-water-park.htm - Travel China Guide pricing and hours

basic_info.name: Dadonghai Beach / 三亞·大東海
basic_info.location: Dadonghai Tourism Zone, between Tuziwei (兔子尾) and Luhuitou (鹿回頭) headlands, Jiyang District, Sanya City, Hainan Province, China; ~3-4 km southeast of Sanya city center, ~28 km / 40 min from Sanya Phoenix International Airport (SYX)
basic_info.operator: Dadonghai Tourism Zone (managed by Sanya municipal authorities; free public beach); historic surf operators include Gravity Surf Club (引力衝浪) and WAVE Surf Club among a small number of in-bay schools; partial water-sports restrictions imposed by Sanya in April 2023 affect specific sub-zones (Broken Bridge / Blue Hole) but the main bay remains accessible
basic_info.opening_date: N/A (natural beach; tourism zone established in the 1980s)

technical_specs.wave_technology_brand: N/A (natural waves)
technical_specs.pool_size: Crescent-shaped bay ~2.9 km long, south-facing between Tuziwei and Luhuitou headlands; main surfable beach extends ~1-2 km along the central sandbar [uncertain]
technical_specs.wave_height: Seasonal average 0.4-1.5 m; summer SW/S swells (May-Oct) can deliver 1.5-2 m peaks on good days; winter (Nov-Apr) typically flat to small (~0.3-0.6 m) due to NE monsoon being blocked by the south-facing orientation
technical_specs.wave_height_adjustability: Not adjustable (natural ocean swell - relies on summer SW monsoon and typhoon swells; very inconsistent)
technical_specs.wave_types: Beach break only - sandy bottom with mellow shore-pound; suitable for white-water beginner waves, occasional clean shoulders during summer south swells; no point or reef break
technical_specs.waves_per_hour: N/A (natural surf spot)
technical_specs.wave_count_per_set: N/A (natural ocean sets, typically 3-5 waves per set with 6-10 second intervals during summer SW swell) [uncertain]
technical_specs.session_length: N/A (open ocean access; surf school lessons typically 90-120 minutes)
technical_specs.stance_rotation_schedule: N/A (beach break - both stances surf the same peaks)
technical_specs.wave_type_classification: Beach break (sandy bottom, gentle gradient, summer south-facing exposure); described in Surf-Forecast as 'fairly exposed' but 'rarely breaks' - suitable mainly for beginner soft-top sessions

pricing_sessions.price_range: Free public beach access; surf lessons typically 500-580 RMB per person for 2 hr beginner session including board, rashguard, sunscreen and often GoPro footage; package of 4 days / 6 lessons (~12 hr) ~2,690 RMB at Gravity Surf Club
pricing_sessions.session_skill_tiers: Largely beginner-focused given inconsistent waves: zero-base trial lesson ~500 RMB; multi-session beginner course ~2,690 RMB / 4 days; intermediate/private lessons on request [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.equipment_rental: Soft-top board ~80-150 RMB/session, ~200-300 RMB/day; rashguard ~30-50 RMB; leash typically included with board hire [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.coaching_fee: Group lesson 500 RMB per person at Gravity Surf (引力衝浪); WAVE Surf Club similar (~500 RMB); private 1-on-1 typically 700-1,000 RMB [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.coaching_packages: Gravity Surf 4-day / 6-lesson package 2,690 RMB (~12 hr total instruction); typical lesson packages bundle GoPro point-of-view footage and drone photos at no extra cost [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.membership_program: Punch cards offered by individual clubs (~2,000-3,500 RMB for 10 sessions); no formal bay-wide membership [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.corporate_team_building: Most surf clubs accept small group / team-building bookings; minimum group sizes vary (typically 6-15 people); specific pricing on request [uncertain]

experience_guide.best_season: May to October (summer SW monsoon and typhoon swell window); inverse to Wanning - summer is the only consistent season; winter is generally flat
experience_guide.best_swell_window: May-Oct: SW/S swells 0.5-1.5 m, period 6-10 s, with occasional typhoon-driven 1.5-2 m days; offshore N winds work best; ideal swell direction is from the south
experience_guide.crowd_level: Beach is highly crowded with tourists year-round, but the surf lineup itself is uncrowded due to inconsistent waves and small surfer population; 2023 Sanya restrictions further reduced commercial water-sports congestion in some sub-zones
experience_guide.booking_method: Walk-in at beachfront surf clubs; online booking via Dianping (大眾點評), Meituan (美團), WeChat mini-programs of operators (Gravity Surf, WAVE Surf), or through Sanya hotel concierges
experience_guide.booking_platform: Dianping (大眾點評), Meituan (美團), WeChat mini-programs, Trip.com / Ctrip (攜程), Klook, Qunar (去哪兒)
experience_guide.payment_options: WeChat Pay and Alipay universally accepted; cash accepted; international credit cards rarely accepted at small surf shops (only at hotels and large beach resorts)
experience_guide.skill_required: Primarily beginner-friendly when waves are present; intermediate surfers will find conditions limiting most days; advanced surfers should head to Riyue Bay (Wanning) instead
experience_guide.age_eligibility: Most clubs accept ages 6+; participants must be able to swim; no formal upper age limit but at instructor discretion [uncertain]
experience_guide.child_friendly_score: 4/5 - calm shallow water for most of the year, soft-top boards, central city location with restrooms / showers / changing rooms; lifeguards on patrol; gentle slope; main hazard is crowded swimming areas mixing with surfers
experience_guide.sup_paddleboard_offering: Yes - SUP, kayak, banana boat and other water sports widely available along Dadonghai beach (subject to 2023 zone restrictions on some commercial activities)
experience_guide.photography_video_service: Most clubs include free GoPro session footage and drone aerial photos in the lesson price; standalone photo packages 100-300 RMB [uncertain]
experience_guide.language_support: Limited English at most surf clubs; some hotel beach concessions and large operators have English-speaking staff; Mandarin and Russian (legacy of Russian tourist boom) more commonly heard
experience_guide.accessibility_for_disabled: No formal adaptive surfing program; beach has paved promenade access in some sections, but no boardwalk to waterline; lifeguard assistance available [uncertain]
experience_guide.stance_preference_input: Not required when booking (beach break suits both stances)
experience_guide.safety_notes: Lifeguards on duty; designated swim/surf zones marked by buoys; April 2023 Sanya notice prohibits unauthorized water tourism in specific sub-areas (Broken Bridge 斷橋, Blue Hole 藍洞 between Dadonghai and Xiaodonghai) - check current zoning before paddling out; main beach surf zones unaffected; watch for jet-skis and banana-boat traffic [uncertain - regulations evolving]
experience_guide.events_competitions_calendar: No major WSL/ISA-tier surf competitions hosted at Dadonghai (those events are at Wanning Riyue Bay); occasional local surf gatherings and beach festivals during summer [uncertain]

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: Dense hotel cluster directly behind the bay: Sanya Marriott Hotel Dadonghai Bay (~700-1,500 RMB), Crowne Plaza Sanya (~600-1,200 RMB), Pullman Oceanview Sanya (~800-1,500 RMB), Sanya Pearl River Garden Hotel (~400-800 RMB), plus dozens of mid-range hotels (~300-700 RMB) and budget guesthouses (~150-300 RMB)
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: No dedicated surf lodge; numerous beachfront hotels (4-5 star) directly across the road from the beach - effectively walk-from-room access to the surf zone
surroundings.nearby_food: Extensive food options: First Market (第一市場) seafood ~10 min drive; Dadonghai commercial street with Hainanese local fare, BBQ stalls, hotpot, Russian/Korean restaurants; per-person spend 50-300 RMB
surroundings.transport: Multiple city buses (lines 2, 8, 15, 16, 24, 25, 26, 28 etc.) connect Dadonghai with Sanya city center, train station and ferry terminal; Didi / taxi readily available; walking distance from many city-center hotels
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: ~28 km / 40 min taxi from Sanya Phoenix International Airport (SYX) (~50 RMB metered); ~12 km / 20 min from Sanya Railway Station; the most accessible Hainan surf spot from a major airport
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Pairs naturally with city Sanya itinerary: Dadonghai surf + Luhuitou Park sunset + Sanya Bay coconut grove + First Market seafood + Yalong Bay or Haitang Bay day trip; or as warm-up before traveling to Wanning Riyue Bay or Houhai for serious surf

pros: Most accessible Hainan surf spot - only 3-4 km from Sanya city center, walkable from many hotels
Free public beach with full city infrastructure (transport, food, lodging at all price points)
Calm, beginner-friendly summer waves with sandy bottom and gentle slope
Warm water year-round (winter ~22 C, summer ~28 C)
Lessons typically include free GoPro and drone footage in the price
cons: Wave consistency poor - winter is mostly flat, summer hit-or-miss; Surf-Forecast describes it as 'rarely breaks'
Beach is heavily crowded with general tourists, jet-skis and swimmers - surf lineup must be navigated carefully
Sand quality is coarser and water clarity inferior to Yalong Bay / Haitang Bay
April 2023 Sanya water-tourism restrictions added regulatory uncertainty in some sub-zones (Broken Bridge / Blue Hole)
Not suitable for intermediate / advanced surfers - serious wave-seekers should head to Riyue Bay or Houhai
reviews_reputation.user_reviews: Trip.com / Ctrip ~4.4/5 for Dadonghai beach overall; Xiaohongshu (小紅書) and Zhihu surf guides describe it as 'convenient but inconsistent' and recommend Houhai Village instead for serious learners; Dianping reviews of Gravity Surf and WAVE Surf clubs generally positive on instructor friendliness but note dependence on weather [uncertain ratings]
reviews_reputation.recommended_for: City-based travelers wanting a one-day surf trial without leaving Sanya; absolute beginners with limited time; families combining beach holiday with a surf taster; corporate groups based at central Sanya hotels
reviews_reputation.operator_brand_partnership: Beach is publicly managed (Sanya Tourism Bureau); main private surf operators include Gravity Surf Club (引力衝浪) and WAVE Surf Club; Sanya Marriott Hotel Dadonghai Bay is the major hospitality anchor

basic_info.name: Fuli Bay (Fuli Wan) / 陵水富力灣 (within Xiangshui Bay / 香水灣)
basic_info.location: Fuli Bay, Yunhaige (云海阁), Guangpo Town (光坡镇), Lingshui Li Autonomous County, Hainan Province, China; located within the larger Xiangshui Bay (Perfume Bay) tourism resort area; ~90 km / 1.5 hr drive northeast of Sanya Phoenix International Airport, ~20 min by high-speed rail to Lingshui Station
basic_info.operator: Natural surf zone fronting the R&F Group (富力集团) Fuli Bay resort development (3,200-mu / ~213 ha integrated resort with Marriott Resort Xiangshui Bay anchor); local surf operators include Hang5 Surf School (the first ASI-accredited surf school in China) and the on-site Xiangshui Bay Marriott surf shop

technical_specs.wave_technology_brand: N/A (natural waves)
technical_specs.pool_size: Natural bay; Fuli Bay sits inside the broader Xiangshui Bay coastline (roughly 6 km of beach), with four distinct surf zones / peaks identified along the Fuli Bay shoreline catering to different ability levels [uncertain]
technical_specs.wave_height: Seasonal average 0.5-1.8 m; winter NE monsoon peaks (Nov-Mar) deliver the largest, most organized sets up to ~2 m; summer SE swells (Mar-Oct) typically 0.4-1.2 m and gentler
technical_specs.wave_height_adjustability: Not adjustable (natural ocean swell driven by NE winter monsoon and SE summer monsoon, with occasional typhoon groundswell)
technical_specs.wave_types: Mix of mellow beach break (sandy bottom, gentle slope) for beginners and a left point setup on the headland for intermediate-to-advanced surfers; described as having four peaks of varying difficulty within the bay
technical_specs.waves_per_hour: N/A (natural surf spot)
technical_specs.wave_count_per_set: N/A (natural ocean sets, typically 3-6 waves per set with 8-14 second intervals depending on swell period) [uncertain]
technical_specs.session_length: N/A (open ocean access; surf school lessons typically 90-120 minutes)
technical_specs.stance_rotation_schedule: N/A (natural break; left point favors goofy frontside, beach break peaks work for both stances)
technical_specs.wave_type_classification: Beach break with adjacent left point break; sandy bottom with gentle gradient makes it one of Hainan's most beginner-tolerant natural spots while still offering quality lines for intermediates

pricing_sessions.price_range: Free open-ocean access; beginner surf lessons at Hang5 and Marriott surf shop typically 380-580 RMB per person (2 hr group, includes board, rashguard, leash); private lessons ~600-900 RMB [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.session_skill_tiers: Hang5 offers Surf Wise certificate progression (Beginner / Improver / Intermediate); group beginner ~380-500 RMB, private 1-on-1 ~600-900 RMB, multi-day camps and ASI-aligned certificate courses on request [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.equipment_rental: Soft-top board rental ~80-150 RMB per session or ~200-300 RMB per day; rashguards ~30-50 RMB; leash typically included with board hire [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.coaching_fee: Group lesson 380-500 RMB per person (2 hr); private 1-on-1 ~600-900 RMB; ASI Level 1 certified instructors available at Hang5 [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.coaching_packages: Multi-day surf camps and Surf Wise certificate packages offered by Hang5 (pricing on request); team-building and corporate programs available; Marriott in-house surf packages bundled with rooms [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.membership_program: 10-session punch cards offered by some local clubs (~2,000-3,000 RMB range); no formal bay-wide membership [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.corporate_team_building: Yes - Hang5 explicitly offers team-building programs; Marriott Resort Xiangshui Bay has 14 event rooms and outdoor spaces and bundles surf-themed corporate experiences; minimum group typically 10+ people [uncertain]

experience_guide.best_season: Year-round surfable; winter (Nov-Mar) is the prime season with consistent NE monsoon swell; summer (Jun-Sep) is one of the best summer surf options in Hainan thanks to SE monsoon and occasional typhoon swell
experience_guide.best_swell_window: Nov-Mar: NE monsoon swells 0.8-2 m, period 7-12 s, offshore W-NW winds; Jun-Sep secondary window with SE swells 0.5-1.2 m and possible typhoon groundswell
experience_guide.crowd_level: Low to moderate; described in Chinese surf media as having a 'wide tolerance zone with very few people' even during winter big-wave days; significantly less crowded than Wanning Riyue Bay or Sanya Houhai
experience_guide.booking_method: Walk-in at the on-site Hang5 Surf School and Marriott surf shop; advance booking via WeChat mini-programs of individual operators, Dianping (大眾點評), Meituan (美團), or through Marriott Resort Xiangshui Bay concierge
experience_guide.booking_platform: Dianping (大眾點評), Meituan (美團), WeChat mini-programs (Hang5 official account), Marriott Bonvoy / Trip.com / Ctrip (攜程) for hotel-bundled surf packages, Xiaohongshu (小紅書) DM for guesthouse-based clubs
experience_guide.payment_options: WeChat Pay and Alipay universally accepted; cash widely accepted; international credit cards accepted at the Marriott resort but rarely at independent surf shops
experience_guide.skill_required: All levels - beginner-friendly inside zones with gentle sand-bottom waves, plus intermediate/advanced peaks at the point; recognized as one of the best Hainan spots for first-timers seeking uncrowded waves
experience_guide.age_eligibility: Hang5 and most clubs accept ages 6+; participants must be able to swim; no formal upper age limit but lessons subject to instructor discretion based on fitness [uncertain]
experience_guide.child_friendly_score: 4/5 - gentle beach gradient, soft-top boards, kids' programs at Hang5; on-site Marriott family facilities; uncrowded lineup reduces collision risk
experience_guide.sup_paddleboard_offering: Yes - SUP rentals and lessons available via the Marriott water sports desk and local operators
experience_guide.photography_video_service: Photo/video service offered by clubs; typical pricing 100-300 RMB for session photos, 300-580 RMB for edited video [uncertain]
experience_guide.language_support: Limited English at most local clubs; Hang5 offers some English-capable instruction (founder is ASI-certified and ASI materials are bilingual); Marriott Resort staff speak English; Mandarin dominant elsewhere
experience_guide.accessibility_for_disabled: No formal adaptive surfing program advertised; Marriott Resort itself has wheelchair-accessible facilities; beach access is sandy [uncertain]
experience_guide.stance_preference_input: Not required when booking; both stances accommodated (beach break peaks suit both, point favors goofy frontside)
experience_guide.safety_notes: Generally calm with gentle gradient; lifeguards present at Marriott beach section but not full bay; ISA / ASI certified coaches available at Hang5; surf insurance recommended; watch for stronger rips during winter big-swell days
experience_guide.events_competitions_calendar: Used as auxiliary national-team training zone; no major WSL/ISA championship events currently scheduled at Fuli Bay (those remain centered at Wanning Riyue Bay) [uncertain]

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: Within the Xiangshui Bay resort cluster: Marriott Resort & Spa Xiangshui Bay (~800-2,000 RMB), Holiday Inn Xiangshui Bay (~600-1,200 RMB), Summer Surfing Resort / Crand Rezen Hotel & Resort (~500-1,000 RMB), Sheraton Shenzhou Peninsula nearby; Hang5 school apartments 200-300 RMB; nearby guesthouses 150-500 RMB
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: Marriott Resort & Spa Xiangshui Bay sits directly behind the Fuli Bay surf zone (north beach surfable from the resort grounds); Hang5 Surf School operates apartments at 200-300 RMB/night including community gym, pool, cinema and sports court access [uncertain pricing]
surroundings.nearby_food: Marriott resort restaurants (Asian, international, all-day dining), Xiangshui Bay commercial street (~25,000 m2 retail strip planned/operating with seafood, Hainanese local fare, cafes), nearby Yingzhou Old Street seafood stalls; per-person spend 50-300 RMB depending on venue
surroundings.transport: Self-drive via G98 East Ring Highway (Lingshui exit); high-speed rail to Lingshui Station then ~20 min taxi/Didi; Didi/online cars and local taxis readily available; resort shuttle from Marriott to nearby attractions
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: ~90 km / ~1.5 hr drive from Sanya Phoenix International Airport (SYX) via G98; ~20 min by high-speed rail from Lingshui Station (~10 km from Fuli Bay) connecting to Sanya in ~30 min and Haikou in ~2 hr
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Common 3-5 day itinerary: Fuli Bay surf + Fenjiezhou Island (分界洲島) snorkeling/diving + Nanwan Monkey Island (南灣猴島) + Xiangshui Bay beach + Wuzhizhou Island day trip; pairs naturally with a Wanning Riyue Bay surf extension (~50 km north)

pros: Recognized in Chinese surf community as 'the best surf spot inside Xiangshui Bay' with very low crowd levels
Year-round surfable with strong summer wave window - one of Hainan's best summer options when Wanning is flat
Very beginner-friendly sandy gradient with adjacent intermediate point peaks for progression
Premium accommodation directly on the wave (Marriott Resort & Spa Xiangshui Bay walk to beach)
First ASI-accredited surf school in China (Hang5) offers internationally recognized instruction
cons: Less developed surf-village infrastructure than Wanning Riyue Bay or Sanya Houhai (fewer shops, bars, surfer cafes)
Travel time from Sanya airport ~1.5 hr discourages short trips
Limited English support outside the Marriott and Hang5
Wave size in summer can be small - not ideal for advanced surfers seeking power
Resort/private-development feel may not appeal to surfers seeking authentic surf-village vibe
reviews_reputation.user_reviews: Xiaohongshu (小紅書) and Zhihu (知乎) surf guides consistently rank Fuli Bay as a 'hidden gem' alternative to Riyue Bay - praised for uncrowded peaks, clean water, and beginner-friendly sandbar; Marriott Resort Xiangshui Bay holds ~4.5/5 on Trip.com / Ctrip; Hang5 Surf School positively reviewed on Dianping for instructor quality [uncertain ratings]
reviews_reputation.recommended_for: Beginners seeking uncrowded learning conditions; intermediate surfers wanting quality lines without Riyue crowds; families combining luxury resort stay with surf lessons; couples; corporate team-building groups
reviews_reputation.operator_brand_partnership: R&F Group (富力集团) developed the 3,200-mu Fuli Bay integrated resort with Marriott International (Marriott Resort & Spa Xiangshui Bay); surf instruction by Hang5 Surf School (ASI / Academy of Surfing Instructors international accreditation - first in China)

basic_info.name: Haikou Fuxing City Wavegarden Cove (海口復興城 Wavegarden Cove) - NOT VERIFIED. No public record - Chinese or international - confirms the existence, construction, or formal announcement of a Wavegarden Cove facility at Haikou Fuxing City as of 2026-05. The original entry appears to conflate the Wavegarden brand with the only operating Hainan wave pool, SURFLAND, which uses American Wave Machines' PerfectSwell technology and is located in Wanning (not Haikou) at Riyue Bay. Fuxing City itself is a digital-economy industrial park, not a leisure or beach destination.
basic_info.location: Allegedly inside or adjacent to Haikou Fuxing City (复兴城). Fuxing City has two campuses: Zone 1 'Fuxing City Internet Innovation Base' (78.6 mu / ~80,000㎡ GFA) at the intersection of Wanlu Park (万绿园), Century Park (世纪公园) and Binhai Park (滨海公园) in Longhua District; Zone 2 'Fuxing City International Digital Port' (256.8 mu / ~900,000㎡ GFA, planned investment ~RMB 10 billion) in the West Coast South District. Both are office / digital-economy parks with no wave-pool component.
basic_info.operator: [uncertain] - No operator identified. No company has been linked to a Wavegarden Cove project at Fuxing City. Fuxing City itself is co-developed under Hainan's 'province + municipality + district + private enterprise' model (Fullsing Group / 复星城产业发展) but no wave-tech licensee has been announced.
basic_info.opening_date: [uncertain] - No opening date because no project is confirmed. WavePoolMag's '2026 New Wave Pools' worldwide list contains no Chinese facility for 2026.

verification.verification_sources: Verification searches conducted 2026-05-15 found no public record of a Wavegarden Cove facility at Haikou Fuxing City. Sources consulted: (1) Baidu Baike entry for 'Haikou Fuxing City Internet Information Industrial Park' confirms it is one of Hainan FTP's 11 key industrial parks, focused on digital economy with four vertical clusters - smart IoT, digital trade, fintech, offshore innovation - and tenants Alibaba, ByteDance, Xiaomi, Schneider, Tesla; no surf / wave-pool / leisure component is listed: https://baike.baidu.com/item/海口复兴城互联网信息产业园/56316328 ; (2) Fuxing City official site park-introduction page describes Zone 1 (78.6 mu / 80,000㎡) and Zone 2 (256.8 mu / ~900,000㎡, ~RMB 10B planned investment) as office and digital-economy infrastructure: http://wap.fullsing.cn/index.php?c=category&id=105 ; (3) Jiemian (界面新闻) feature on Fuxing City's four vertical industry clusters and FTP positioning - no aquatic-leisure mention: https://www.jiemian.com/article/4172318.html ; (4) The Paper 'Haikou Fuxing City 2.0' coverage describes Zone 2 expansion as digital industry hub - no wave pool: https://m.thepaper.cn/newsDetail_forward_15598142 ; (5) The Paper 'towards trillion-yuan digital economy cluster' coverage on Fuxing City - again purely tech / digital, no surf: https://m.thepaper.cn/newsDetail_forward_16898883 ; (6) 21Jingji on Fuxing City Cross-Border Data Service Industry Base - tech/data focus only: https://www.21jingji.com/article/20240822/herald/5046b756cf3aa608344b3a90a2d4c4ca.html ; (7) Wavegarden's official 2026 'Six new world-class surf destinations' announcement names DSRT Surf USA, El Nido Uruguay, Surfers Cove Portugal, Gemswell Surf Madrid Spain, Perth Surf Park Australia, Auckland Surf Park NZ - no Chinese / Hainan project: https://amusementtoday.com/2026/04/6-new-wavegarden-world-class-surf-destinations-on-the-horizon/ ; (8) Wavegarden corporate site (https://wavegarden.com/) carries no Hainan press release as of 2026-05; (9) WavePoolMag 'New Wave Pools 2026' index lists every wave pool opening worldwide in 2026 - no Chinese facility appears: https://wavepoolmag.com/new-wave-pools-2026/ ; (10) The only confirmed operational artificial wave pool in Hainan as of 2026-05 is SURFLAND at Wanning Riyue Bay, using PerfectSwell technology (NOT Wavegarden Cove) - this is the project the entry is most likely confused with. Sources: TropicalHainan https://www.tropicalhainan.com/chinas-first-olympic-standard-wave-pool-opens-in-wanning-hainan/ , Wavepool Mag https://wavepoolmag.com/wave-pool-china-perfectswell-hainan/ , Korea Herald https://www.koreaherald.com/article/10623347 , Surfway Hainan official https://surfway-hainan.com/. SURFLAND is in Wanning, ~60 km from Haikou. Conclusion: as of 2026-05-15 no Wavegarden Cove facility exists, is under construction, or has been formally announced at Haikou Fuxing City. The original entry appears to conflate Wavegarden with the PerfectSwell SURFLAND project in Wanning and incorrectly assigns it to the Fuxing City digital-economy park in Haikou.

basic_info.name: Sanya Haitang Bay Surf Town (三亞海棠灣 Surf Town) - NOT VERIFIED as a distinct project. The only existing 'surf town' concept in Haitang Bay refers to Hou Hai Village (後海村), a natural surf village often called 'China's First Surf Village', with a planned 'Surf Tourism Town' (冲浪旅游风情小镇) tourism development - NOT an artificial wave pool. The genuine operating artificial wave pool surf town in Hainan is SURFLAND at Wanning Riyue Bay, not Haitang Bay.
basic_info.location: Haitang Bay, Sanya, Hainan (三亞·海棠灣). The proposed/referenced area corresponds to Hou Hai Village (後海村, also known as Tenghai Fishing Village 藤海漁村), located at the southern end of Haitang Bay near Wuzhizhou Island. No specific artificial wave pool site address exists.
