basic_info.name: Houhai Village (Houhai Bay) / 三亞·後海村 (后海湾)
basic_info.location: Houhai Village, Tenghai Community, Haitang Bay Town, Haitang District, Sanya City, Hainan Province; ~45 km northeast of Sanya downtown, adjacent to Wuzhizhou Island ferry terminal
basic_info.operator: Self-organized surf village; no single operator; 70+ surf clubs and guesthouses run independently; pioneer instructor Yang Xingfu (楊興福, ISA-certified, started teaching 2015) considered founder of village surf scene; managed under Haitang District / Sanya Tourism Board
basic_info.opening_date: N/A (natural surf spot; surf scene developed organically since ~2008-2015)

technical_specs.wave_technology_brand: N/A (natural waves)
technical_specs.pool_size: Crescent-shaped sheltered bay extends ~2-3 km of beach; main surf zone ~500 m wide [uncertain]
technical_specs.wave_height: Seasonal average 0.3-1.5 m; gentle year-round (avg 0.5-1 m); larger 1-2.5 m during typhoon swells (Aug-Oct) and winter NE swells
technical_specs.wave_height_adjustability: Not adjustable (natural ocean swell); naturally small and forgiving due to bay's sheltered geometry
technical_specs.wave_types: Beach break over sand with both left and right peelers; mostly soft, mellow whitewater and shoulder waves ideal for beginners; small clean waves suit longboard cruising; rare bigger swells offer intermediate face waves
technical_specs.waves_per_hour: N/A (natural surf spot)
technical_specs.wave_count_per_set: N/A (natural ocean sets, typically 2-4 waves per set with 6-10 second intervals) [uncertain]
technical_specs.session_length: N/A (open ocean access; surf school lessons typically 120 minutes)
technical_specs.stance_rotation_schedule: N/A (beach break with both lefts and rights; no daily rotation needed)
technical_specs.wave_type_classification: Beach break over sand; sheltered, year-round mellow waves; ideal for soft-top longboards and beginner foamies

pricing_sessions.price_range: Free open-ocean access; surf lessons typically 200-400 RMB per session including board and rashguard; private lessons 320-500 RMB
pricing_sessions.session_skill_tiers: Group lesson (3 people, 2 hours) ~130 RMB + 5 RMB insurance per person; standard group ~200-300 RMB; private 1-on-1 ~320-500 RMB; intensive multi-day camps 800-2,000 RMB
pricing_sessions.equipment_rental: Surfboard rental ~100 RMB/day; rashguard often included; daily packages with board until 7 PM common at most shops; wetsuit rarely needed but available ~40-50 RMB
pricing_sessions.coaching_fee: Group lesson 200-400 RMB per person (2 hours); private lesson ~320-500 RMB; package often includes board rental until 7 PM, rashguard, face zinc, GoPro video
pricing_sessions.coaching_packages: Day pass (lesson + board until 7 PM + rashguard + GoPro footage) ~300-400 RMB; multi-day camps 1,500-3,000 RMB; couple/family packages negotiable [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.membership_program: Many shops sell 10-session punch cards at ~1,500-2,500 RMB; informal monthly memberships at popular clubs [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.corporate_team_building: Numerous surf clubs accept team-building groups; common minimums 10-20 people; pricing 200-400 RMB per person plus group catering [uncertain]

experience_guide.best_season: Year-round surfable (~200+ surfable days); best gentle waves April-October for beginners; larger waves November-March (NE monsoon) and August-September (typhoon swells)
experience_guide.best_swell_window: Year-round mild swells: NE-E winter swells (Nov-Mar) bring cleanest 0.8-1.5 m days; SE/S summer swells (May-Oct) provide gentle small waves; typhoon groundswells Aug-Oct can deliver 1.5-2.5 m days (beach may close for safety)
experience_guide.crowd_level: Very crowded in peak winter (Nov-Feb) and Chinese holidays (National Day, Spring Festival) with hundreds of beginners simultaneously; moderate weekday crowds in summer; early morning (6-8 AM) least crowded
experience_guide.booking_method: Walk-in at any beachfront surf club; online via Dianping (大眾點評), Meituan, Xiaohongshu, WeChat mini-programs of individual schools, or Trip.com/Ctrip
experience_guide.booking_platform: Dianping (大眾點評), Meituan (美團), Xiaohongshu (小紅書), Trip.com / Ctrip (攜程), WeChat mini-programs
experience_guide.payment_options: WeChat Pay and Alipay universally accepted; cash accepted; international credit cards rarely accepted at village-level shops
experience_guide.skill_required: Zero-base beginners welcome and form majority of users; intermediate surfers also enjoy small-day longboarding; not suitable for advanced surfers seeking power
experience_guide.age_eligibility: Most schools accept kids 6+ with parental supervision; no upper age limit; participants must be able to swim or wear flotation
experience_guide.child_friendly_score: 5/5 - shallow sandy bay where you can walk far out and still touch sand; gentle waves; many schools offer dedicated kids lessons and soft-top boards; family-friendly environment
experience_guide.sup_paddleboard_offering: Yes - SUP rentals and lessons widely available at most surf clubs
experience_guide.photography_video_service: Many lesson packages include free GoPro session footage; standalone photo/video service ~100-300 RMB; drone shoots ~300-500 RMB [uncertain]
experience_guide.language_support: Limited English; some clubs (e.g., foreign-frequented shops) have English-speaking coaches; Mandarin dominant; Russian heard at some shops
experience_guide.accessibility_for_disabled: No formal adaptive surfing program; sandy beach access; some surf hostels have ground-floor rooms but not certified accessible [uncertain]
experience_guide.stance_preference_input: Not required when booking; both lefts and rights available so stance is irrelevant
experience_guide.safety_notes: Generally safe shallow sandy bottom; main hazards are crowding (board collisions), strong sun, and typhoon-period closures; lifeguards on main beach during peak hours; some schools provide insurance for ~5 RMB; ISA-certified coaches at top clubs (founder Yang Xingfu and others)
experience_guide.events_competitions_calendar: Hosts smaller-scale Hainan Provincial qualifiers, surf festivals, and brand events (e.g., Vans, Quiksilver demos); no major WSL/ISA tour stop (those are at Riyue Bay); annual surf-village beach parties [uncertain]

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: 70+ surf-themed guesthouses and hostels in Houhai Village ranging 80-300 RMB (hostel dorms) to 300-700 RMB (private rooms); examples: Elaine Seaview Surf Design Guesthouse from ~$12 (~85 RMB), Jile Surfing Inn from ~$20 (~140 RMB), Yunhai Jingqi Surf Hostel from ~$29 (~210 RMB); average one-night stay ~$47 (~340 RMB)
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: Sanya Houhai Surfing Sea View Resort Homestay; Homecoming Seascape Surfing Vacation; numerous surf-school operated guesthouses with packages combining lodging + lessons [uncertain pricing details]
surroundings.nearby_food: Tanka seafood (freshly caught with yellow chili and sour orange dip), Hainanese snacks (qingbuliang 清補涼, baoluo noodles 抱羅粉), street BBQ, fresh coconut, SASA de Sea (local cuisine), Surf House Cafe, fishing village night market; per-person spend 50-150 RMB
surroundings.transport: Self-drive ~40 minutes from Sanya downtown; Didi/taxi widely available; Bus 28 to Wuzhizhou Island stop + 10-min walk; high-speed train to Yalongwan Station (~110 RMB from Sanya) then Didi ~30 RMB
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: ~50 km / ~50-60 minutes drive from Sanya Phoenix International Airport (SYX); ~30 minutes from Sanya central station; ~10 minutes from Wuzhizhou Island ferry; high-speed rail to Yalongwan Station (~110 RMB) + 30-min Didi
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Houhai surf + Wuzhizhou Island (蜈支洲島) snorkeling + Haitang Bay duty-free shopping + Yalong Bay beach + Atlantis Sanya resort; 2-4 day Sanya beach + surf itinerary

pros: China's most beginner-friendly surf spot with shallow, sheltered bay and gentle waves
Highest density of surf shops/clubs (70+) in China; easy walk-in lessons and gear
Year-round surfable conditions (~200+ days/year)
Vibrant fishing-village atmosphere with cafes, bars, night market, and beach parties
Affordable accommodation and lessons; budget-friendly compared to Riyue Bay or international spots
cons: Extremely crowded in peak season; collisions and queueing in lineup common
Waves too small/soft for intermediate-advanced surfers seeking power or barrels
Surf school quality varies wildly; many inexperienced coaches with quick certification
Increasing commercialization eroding original fishing-village charm
Limited English; foreign visitors must rely on translation apps
reviews_reputation.user_reviews: Trip.com / Tripadvisor: 4.0-4.5/5; Xiaohongshu (小紅書) features thousands of viral 'Houhai surf girl' posts making it a top Chinese surf destination; Dianping shows generally positive reviews praising vibe and accessibility but flagging crowding and inconsistent coaching quality; Lonely Planet describes it as a relaxed laid-back village
reviews_reputation.recommended_for: Beginners and first-time surfers; family/parent-child trips; couples seeking surf+nightlife combo; social media/photo punters; longboarders; surf-vacation groups; budget travelers
reviews_reputation.operator_brand_partnership: Sanya Tourism Board promotes Houhai as official surf village destination; pioneer school Yang Xingfu Surf Club ISA-affiliated; brand demos by Vans, Quiksilver, Roxy at periodic events; no single corporate operator [uncertain]

basic_info.name: Jielangbu Surf Club / JALENBOO Surf Club (戒浪不沖浪俱樂部, also written 戒浪·不; English brand: JALENBOO; previously Riyue Bay Surf Club / Surfing Hainan)
basic_info.location: Riyue Bay (Sun & Moon Bay), Liji Town, Wanning City, Hainan Province, China - the original Riyue Bay flagship store sits on the main surf beach; additional branches in Qingshui Bay (清水灣, Hainan) and Shilaoren (石老人, Qingdao, Shandong)
basic_info.operator: Hainan Wanning Riyue Bay Surf Service Co., Ltd. (海南萬寧日月灣衝浪服務有限公司); legal representative Zeng Shan (曾山); registered capital 1.25 million RMB; ~33 employees as of 2023; supported by the Wanning municipal government under a 'government + enterprise' surf-industry model
basic_info.opening_date: 2011-12-30 (parent company registered 2011-08-31; Riyue Bay flagship opened 2011-12-30; rebranded from 'Riyue Bay Surf Club' to 'JALENBOO / 戒浪不')

technical_specs.wave_technology_brand: N/A - natural ocean surf spot (not an artificial wave pool); coaching delivered on the natural waves of Riyue Bay
technical_specs.pool_size: Operates along the ~2 km natural Riyue Bay surf beach (not a wave pool)
technical_specs.wave_height: Roughly 0.5-2.5 m seasonal range at Riyue Bay; ~0.5-1.0 m soft/peeling whitewater inside zone for beginners; up to 2-3 m faces during the October-March NE monsoon swell window
technical_specs.wave_height_adjustability: No - natural waves; the school selects coaching zones (whitewater inside vs. green-wave outside) and date windows to match student level
technical_specs.wave_types: Natural beach break with reef-influenced sections; soft whitewater for beginners, peeling shoulders for intermediate, occasional steeper faces and short barrel sections during peak NE swell for advanced
technical_specs.waves_per_hour: N/A - natural ocean surf, wave count depends on swell and tide
technical_specs.wave_count_per_set: N/A - natural ocean swell sets, typically 3-6 waves per set with 8-15 second intervals depending on swell period [uncertain]
technical_specs.session_length: 120 minutes (2-hour group lesson is the standard format)
technical_specs.stance_rotation_schedule: N/A - natural beach break, no stance rotation; both Goofy and Regular work equally
technical_specs.wave_type_classification: Natural beach break / point-style peel at Riyue Bay - longboard-friendly, suitable for soft-tops in beginner zone and shortboards on the outside

pricing_sessions.price_range: Group lesson approximately 400 RMB per session (2 hours); 3-session package ~1,080 RMB; price typically includes coaching, board and leash use during the lesson
pricing_sessions.session_skill_tiers: Primary tiers: Beginner group class (1 coach to up to 3 students, 2 hours, ~400 RMB/person); multi-class package (3 sessions ~1,080 RMB); private and progression classes available on request [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.equipment_rental: Soft-top and hard-top surfboard, leash and rashguard typically bundled with the lesson; standalone board rental ~80-150 RMB/day; wetsuit rental ~40-80 RMB/day [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.coaching_fee: Group coaching ~400 RMB/2-hour session (1:3 ratio max); private 1-on-1 coaching available at higher rate [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.coaching_packages: 3-class bundle ~1,080 RMB; multi-class cards can be shared across people and across the Riyue Bay, Qingshui Bay and Qingdao branches (拼課 / 拼卡)
pricing_sessions.membership_program: Class-card / multi-session card system that allows pooling between students and use across all three Jielangbu branches; specific tier pricing not published online [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.corporate_team_building: Yes - the club hosts events, school/university training camps and corporate group bookings; specific minimum group size and pricing handled case by case [uncertain]

experience_guide.best_season: October to March is the prime surf season at Riyue Bay (NE monsoon swell, water ~22-26 C); April-September is smaller and more beginner-friendly; club operates year-round
experience_guide.best_swell_window: October to March (NE monsoon); typhoon swells in summer/early autumn can also produce quality waves
experience_guide.crowd_level: Riyue Bay has ~33 surf clubs and 170+ instructors as of 2023, making the lineup busy on weekends, Spring Festival, May Day and National Day; weekday mornings (9-11) and afternoons (15-17) are calmer; 2021 was peak with up to 4 full classes/day at Jielangbu
experience_guide.booking_method: Reserve via the official website, Taobao store, WeChat (official account / mini-program), or via OTA listings on Ctrip / Dianping; walk-ins also accepted at the Riyue Bay store
experience_guide.booking_platform: Official site jalenboo.com; Taobao (淘寶) flagship store; WeChat official account; Dianping (大眾點評); Ctrip / Trip.com (攜程); Xiaohongshu (小紅書) DM
experience_guide.payment_options: Alipay, WeChat Pay, Chinese bank cards and cash; international cards not consistently supported [uncertain]
experience_guide.skill_required: All levels - school is best known as a zero-base beginner entry point; also runs intermediate and progression coaching
experience_guide.age_eligibility: Generally accepts students from ~6 years old upward with a parent/guardian for minors; upper age limit flexible based on health and swimming ability [uncertain]
experience_guide.child_friendly_score: 4 out of 5 - dedicated kids' surf camps, soft-top equipment, gentle inside whitewater zone; kids' programs run during summer and Chinese national holidays
experience_guide.sup_paddleboard_offering: Limited - core focus is surfing; SUP rentals available informally on the bay through partner operators [uncertain]
experience_guide.photography_video_service: Lesson photo / video coverage offered as add-on; specific pricing not published online [uncertain]
experience_guide.language_support: Mandarin Chinese is primary; some coaches and front-desk staff handle basic English; foreign-language depth is below dedicated foreigner-facing schools [uncertain]
experience_guide.accessibility_for_disabled: No publicly listed adaptive surfing program or barrier-free in-water lesson [uncertain]
experience_guide.stance_preference_input: Not required at booking - natural beach break works for both Goofy and Regular; coaches assess stance during the land warm-up
experience_guide.safety_notes: Mandatory pre-lesson land briefing and warm-up; helmets and leashes provided; lifeguards on the bay; coaching team includes long-time local surfers who help train candidates for the China national surf squad and have produced national-championship-level athletes (e.g. Dong Yao, men's shortboard champion at the 2024 Hainan Province Surf Championships); not all coaches hold the international ISA certification - industry-wide ISA-certified coverage in Riyue Bay is reported at under 5%, so verify the specific instructor's credentials when booking [uncertain]
experience_guide.events_competitions_calendar: Long-running organizer of Riyue Bay surf events including the 'Riyue Bay World Surf Day' (drew nearly 10,000 participants by 2023), Hainan Open and the early XSories Jalenboo Surf and Skate Competition (since 2013); supports national-team trials and provincial championships

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: Riyue Bay surf village offers 50+ guesthouses and surf hostels priced ~250-1,200 RMB/night; on-bay options include Sweetome Surf Hotel and Forest Inn Riyue Bay Surf Branch; the new SURFLAND Wanning Surf Resort (Yue Chao + Yu Yue, 132 rooms) sits adjacent to the bay with rates from ~780 RMB/night
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: Jielangbu operates surf-themed lodging at its Riyue Bay base with private rooms and dorm-style hostel beds for surf students; meals and accommodation are included for staff and long-stay students [uncertain]
surroundings.nearby_food: Riyue Bay surf village has surf-cafe brunch spots, Hainanese seafood restaurants, hotpot, and beachfront bars; the SURFLAND X-LIVIN Street inside the adjacent resort adds upscale cafes, restaurants and a live-music venue
surroundings.transport: Self-drive, Didi ride-hailing, taxi or club shuttle from Wanning city, Sanya or Haikou; many travelers take Hainan high-speed rail to Shenzhou Station and connect by 20-minute taxi
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: ~70 minutes by car from Sanya Phoenix International Airport (SYX); ~20 minutes from Shenzhou high-speed rail station; ~2 hours from Haikou Meilan International Airport (HAK)
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Half-day surf at Riyue Bay + half-day pool session at SURFLAND PerfectSwell (next door) + Xinglong Tropical Botanical Garden + Shenzhou Peninsula day trip + Wanning city seafood dinner; multi-day learn-to-surf packages popular with university clubs and youth camps

reviews_reputation.pros: 1) One of the longest-running and largest surf clubs in China (since 2011), strong brand recognition; 2) Multi-branch network (Wanning, Qingshui Bay, Qingdao) lets students reuse class cards across cities; 3) Pricing is mid-market and transparent (~400 RMB / 2-hour group class); 4) Strong competition pedigree - has trained athletes who reached provincial championship and national-team levels; 5) Widely recommended on Zhihu, Xiaohongshu and travel guides as a safer alternative to ad-hoc 'beach freelance' coaches
reviews_reputation.cons: 1) Riyue Bay lineup is very crowded during NE swell season and Chinese holidays; 2) Like much of the Riyue Bay industry, only a small share of coaches hold international ISA certification - quality varies by individual instructor; 3) English support is limited compared with foreigner-focused schools (e.g. Surfway Hainan); 4) Local press coverage in 2024 ('Riyue Bay is no longer a surf utopia') notes price competition and overcrowding has eroded the village's relaxed vibe; 5) Limited transparent online pricing for private lessons, photos and accommodation packages
reviews_reputation.user_reviews: Generally positive on Dianping, Xiaohongshu, Zhihu and travel blogs - praised as Riyue Bay's most established and reliable surf school, considered safer and more professional than informal beach freelance coaches; Riyue Bay scenic area itself rates ~4.9 on Dianping; criticism centers on holiday-season crowding, varying coach skill levels, and the broader bay becoming more commercial
reviews_reputation.recommended_for: Zero-base beginners and intermediate surfers from mainland China; university student groups and youth surf camps; travelers who want a long-running brand with class-card flexibility across multiple cities; competitive surfers preparing for Hainan Province Championships
reviews_reputation.operator_brand_partnership: Hainan Wanning Riyue Bay Surf Service Co., Ltd. (private company, founded 2011) - early partner of XSories for the 2013 XSories Jalenboo Surf and Skate Competition; supported by Wanning municipal government as part of Hainan's surf-industry promotion; serves as a practice base for Chinese university leisure-sports programs and feeds athletes into the China national surf team training pool

verification.verification_sources: 1) Baidu Baike entry for 日月湾冲浪俱乐部 (founding date, legal representative, registered capital, 3,000+ trained members, sub-brand JALENBOO); 2) Trip.com / Ctrip listing for 戒浪不沖浪俱樂部（日月灣店）attraction id 80246727; 3) Jalenboo official site jalenboo.com; 4) Tripadvisor 'Riyue Bay Surf Club' page (Wanning, Hainan); 5) Zhihu article 'Hainan surf clubs - 10 high-popularity recommendations' (zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/369288263); 6) Zhihu article 'Hainan surfing - this one article is enough' (zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/617339364); 7) KickerClub 2013 coverage of the XSories Jalenboo 戒浪.不 Surf and Skate Competition; 8) Tencent News 2024 feature '日月湾，不再是冲浪乌托邦' (industry context and pricing); 9) Driftwoodrhythms travel guide 'Riyue Bay - China's Surfing Capital'

basic_info.name: Jiyue & 8MM Surf Clubs (亼楽 / 集楽 / Jile Surf Hostel & 8MM Surf Club) - two of the original co-located surf-hostel brands at Houhai Village, Sanya
basic_info.location: Houhai Village (后海村, also Tenghai Fishing Village 藤海漁村), Haitang Bay (海棠灣) area, north of Queens Bay (皇后灣), Sanya City, Hainan Province, China; both clubs front the Queens Bay surf beach. 8MM is registered at Tenghai Community West Village No. 70 (海棠灣騰海社區西村70號)
basic_info.operator: Independent surf hostels / clubs - 亼楽 (Jiyue / Jile Surf Hostel) is widely cited as the village's earliest opened and most atmospheric surf hostel; 8MM is a separate established surf-hostel brand on the same Queens Bay beachfront; both are privately owned and unaffiliated with national chains
basic_info.opening_date: Both clubs are part of Houhai's first wave of surf hostels (mid-2010s); 亼楽 is repeatedly described as 'the earliest opened surf hostel in the village'; exact founding year not publicly listed [uncertain]

technical_specs.wave_technology_brand: N/A - natural ocean surf at Queens Bay (皇后灣), Houhai
technical_specs.pool_size: Operates along the ~1.5 km Queens Bay natural surf beach in Houhai (not a wave pool)
technical_specs.wave_height: Average ~1 m year-round, with most days in the 0.5-1.5 m soft / mellow whitewater-to-shoulder range; occasional 1.5-2 m days during stronger SE/E swells - explicitly recommended as a beginner-friendly zone
technical_specs.wave_height_adjustability: No - natural waves; the schools choose inside whitewater vs. outside green-wave zones based on student level
technical_specs.wave_types: Natural beach break with crescent-shaped bay geometry; gentle, slow-peeling 'baby waves' rolling in from the east, very consistent and forgiving - ideal for beginners and improving intermediates
technical_specs.waves_per_hour: N/A - natural ocean surf
technical_specs.wave_count_per_set: N/A - natural ocean swell sets [uncertain]
technical_specs.session_length: Standard private class is one session (~2 hours including land briefing and water time) [uncertain]
technical_specs.stance_rotation_schedule: N/A - natural beach break works for both Goofy and Regular
technical_specs.wave_type_classification: Natural beach break - rated one of China's top beginner-friendly breaks; longboard / soft-top dominant; year-round waves so suitable for first-timers any month

pricing_sessions.price_range: 亼楽: 1-on-1 private lesson ~500 RMB per session. 8MM: 1-on-4 group lesson 580 RMB per person, with discount for full 4-person bookings; equipment typically included for the lesson
pricing_sessions.session_skill_tiers: 亼楽: 1-on-1 private 500 RMB; adult and kids' multi-day surf camps (lower per-day rate, less individualized attention). 8MM: 1-on-4 small-group 580 RMB/person (4-person discount available); private 1-on-1 available at higher rate [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.equipment_rental: Surfboard, leash and rashguard typically included in the lesson fee; standalone soft-top or longboard rental at Houhai shops generally ~80-150 RMB/day; wetsuit rarely needed (water 24-28 C most of the year) [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.coaching_fee: 亼楽 1-on-1 private 500 RMB; 8MM 1-on-4 small-group 580 RMB/person; both clubs use experienced long-time local surfers as coaches
pricing_sessions.coaching_packages: Multi-day surf camps for adults and kids (亼楽); 4-person group package discount (8MM); long-stay surf-and-stay packages with on-site rooms common at both [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.membership_program: No formally published membership / annual card; both clubs operate as surf-hostels where stay-and-surf packages effectively serve as the loyalty model; long-term volunteer (義工) program at 亼楽 grants free room and surf in exchange for hostel/cafe duties
pricing_sessions.corporate_team_building: Both clubs accept group and team-building bookings; specific minimum size and pricing not published online [uncertain]

experience_guide.best_season: Year-round - Houhai / Queens Bay has consistent waves almost every day of the year thanks to the crescent bay geometry; peak surf months are roughly October to April (NE monsoon); summer is smaller and most beginner-friendly
experience_guide.best_swell_window: October to April for the most consistent rideable swells; year-round for beginner-level waves
experience_guide.crowd_level: Houhai is now branded 'China's No. 1 surf village' with 100+ surf shops and 50+ guesthouses; Queens Bay lineup is very crowded on weekends, Spring Festival, May Day and National Day; weekday early mornings and late afternoons are best to avoid the rush
experience_guide.booking_method: Direct via WeChat (official accounts of each hostel), Xiaohongshu DM, Dianping reservation, Ctrip / Trip.com hostel listing (especially for Jile Surf Hostel), or in-person walk-up; surf lessons usually bundled when booking the room
experience_guide.booking_platform: WeChat / WeChat mini-program; Xiaohongshu (小紅書); Dianping (大眾點評); Ctrip / Trip.com / 攜程 (Jile Surf Hostel listed); Tripadvisor (Jile Surf Hostel listed in English); Meituan (美團) for some packages
experience_guide.payment_options: Alipay, WeChat Pay, Chinese bank cards and cash; international card support is limited [uncertain]
experience_guide.skill_required: All levels - both clubs are best known as zero-base beginner entry points; intermediate students also welcome
experience_guide.age_eligibility: Children's surf camps offered (亼楽); typical minimum age ~6-7 with parental waiver; no formal upper age limit beyond health/swim ability [uncertain]
experience_guide.child_friendly_score: 4 out of 5 - 亼楽 explicitly runs kids' surf camps; gentle Queens Bay waves are well-suited to children; minor downsides are the busy beach scene and limited dedicated kids' facilities
experience_guide.sup_paddleboard_offering: SUP and beach rentals widely available in Houhai village through partner shops and adjacent operators; not a marquee offering of either club specifically [uncertain]
experience_guide.photography_video_service: Lesson photo and short video coverage offered informally by coaches and partner photographers; pricing varies and is not published online [uncertain]
experience_guide.language_support: Mandarin primary; some basic English handled by younger staff at the cafe-bar; not a foreigner-focused school [uncertain]
experience_guide.accessibility_for_disabled: No publicly listed adaptive surfing program or barrier-free in-water lesson [uncertain]
experience_guide.stance_preference_input: Not required at booking - natural beach break works for both Goofy and Regular; coaches assess stance during land warm-up
experience_guide.safety_notes: Both clubs are praised for patient, experienced coaching staff; mandatory land briefing and warm-up; soft-top boards and leashes for beginners; lifeguard coverage on Queens Bay; note that across Houhai overall, fewer than ~5% of coaches hold international ISA certification, so verify specific instructor credentials when booking [uncertain]
experience_guide.events_competitions_calendar: Houhai hosts informal community surf jams and pool/beach parties (8MM is known for occasional pool parties at its bar); large WSL/ISA sanctioned events are concentrated at Riyue Bay, not Houhai [uncertain]

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: Houhai village hosts 50+ surf guesthouses and hostels at ~300-1,200 RMB/night; clean comfortable rooms typically around 400 RMB; both 亼楽 and 8MM are themselves surf-hostels with on-site rooms
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: Yes - 亼楽 (Jile Surf Hostel) operates as a surf-themed hostel with sea-view rooms, sofa lounge, video-coaching area, bar and open kitchen; 8MM operates as a surf-club hostel with restaurant and a swimming pool used for evening pool parties; many students choose to stay on-site while taking the surf course
surroundings.nearby_food: Houhai surf village offers a dense cluster of surf-cafe brunch spots, Hainanese seafood restaurants, beach BBQ stalls, hotpot and beach bars; 8MM is specifically called out as having 'better food' and a lively bar scene
surroundings.transport: Reachable by Didi / taxi from Sanya city (~45-60 min), self-drive, hostel shuttle, or Sanya bus routes (Haitang Bay route 2, 33, 23, Lingji 3) to nearby stops including Dongxi Village junction, Nantian Farm, Tengqiao Market and Nantian Depot
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: ~45 km / 60-75 minutes by car from Sanya Phoenix International Airport (SYX); ~30 minutes from Sanya city center; ~5-10 minutes from Haitang Bay high-end resort cluster (Atlantis Sanya etc.)
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Half-day surf at Queens Bay (Houhai) + Atlantis Sanya / Haitang Bay shopping & dining + Wuzhizhou Island day trip + Sanya old-town night market; multi-day surf camp at 亼楽 / 8MM popular with young travelers from mainland China

reviews_reputation.pros: 1) Located on Queens Bay - one of China's most beginner-friendly natural surf breaks with year-round waves; 2) Surf-hostel format means students can stay on-site and surf at the doorstep; 3) Both clubs use experienced long-time local surfer coaches and are praised for patience and good attitude; 4) 亼楽 has the village's longest-running brand reputation and best 'vibe'; 8MM scores highly for food, bar scene and pool parties; 5) Mid-market pricing (500-580 RMB / lesson) typical of established Houhai operators
reviews_reputation.cons: 1) Houhai is now extremely crowded - the village has 100+ surf shops and weekend / holiday lineups can be unpleasant; 2) Industry-wide ISA-certified coach coverage is below ~5%, so quality varies by individual instructor; 3) Limited English support compared to foreigner-focused schools; 4) Beach scene can be hectic and party-oriented (especially around 8MM), which may not suit families seeking a quiet stay; 5) Ad-hoc pricing for photos, private upgrades and accommodation - little published online
reviews_reputation.user_reviews: Both clubs are repeatedly recommended in major Chinese travel guides (Zhihu, Xiaohongshu, Sohu, Smzdm) as among the more reputable Houhai surf hostels; 亼楽 (Jile Surf Hostel) has positive Tripadvisor reviews highlighting beachfront pool, cocktail bar and friendly atmosphere; 8MM is frequently cited for great food, lively pool parties and patient veteran coaches; common criticism is overcrowding of Houhai overall and the broader concern that Houhai 'is no longer the secluded fishing village it was'
reviews_reputation.recommended_for: Zero-base beginners (especially solo travelers and young groups from mainland China); surf-and-stay travelers wanting the hostel/social scene; families with kids 6+ for kids' surf camps (亼楽); social travelers looking for evening bar / pool-party vibe (8MM)
reviews_reputation.operator_brand_partnership: Independent surf hostels rather than chain brands; both are part of Houhai's first generation of surf-hostel pioneers (alongside Jile Surfing Club / Jix Surf Club founded 2014 by Wu Zhu); together they helped position Houhai / Tenghai Fishing Village as Sanya's flagship surf community and a Sanya Tourism Board promoted destination

verification.verification_sources: 1) Zhihu article 'Hainan surfing - this one article is enough' (zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/617339364) - explicitly lists 亼楽 1-on-1 private 500 RMB and 8MM 1-on-4 580 RMB/person; 2) Zhihu article 'Hainan surf clubs - 10 high-popularity recommendations' (zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/369288263); 3) Smzdm reposted version (post.smzdm.com/p/arqk43eq); 4) City8 Sanya local listing for 8MM 冲浪俱乐部 at Tenghai Community West Village No. 70; 5) Tripadvisor Jile Surf Hostel listing (Sanya, Hainan, hotel id g297427-d6933019); 6) Sanya Tourism Board page for Houhai Village (visitsanya.com/destination/houhai-village); 7) The Paper / 澎湃 feature 'I'm in Sanya Houhai Village - surfing, hostels and drifters'; 8) China News (chinanews) 2024 feature on Houhai 'breaking out' as a surf destination; 9) ChinaWondersGuide article on Houhai Village surfing and nightlife

basic_info.name: Ocean Flower Island Water Kingdom / Children's World Water Park (海花岛童世界水上王国)
basic_info.location: South side of Snow Mountain Ocean Zone, Island No. 1, Ocean Flower Island, Binhai New District, Danzhou City, Hainan Province, China (海南省儋州市滨海新区海花岛1号岛雪山海洋区南侧)
basic_info.operator: Hengda Children's World Group (恒大童世界集团) via Danzhou Changyu Tourism Development Co., Ltd. (儋州昌宇旅游开发有限公司); originally developed by Evergrande Group (恒大集团) on Ocean Flower Island master-plan
basic_info.opening_date: 2021-06-01 (initial trial opening); reopened 2023-03-24 after upgrade; relaunched again 2024-04-20 after second renovation

technical_specs.wave_technology_brand: Wave system from major international waterpark equipment supplier (park designed by WTI - WhiteWater Tube & Inflatables / WTI Design); slides and racer mats supplied by ProSlide, WhiteWater West, Dalang, and Guangdong H-Fun [uncertain on specific wave generator brand]
technical_specs.pool_size: Total water park area approximately 110,000 m² (some sources cite 130,000 m²); central wave pool 'Hurricane Wave Bay' (飓浪谷) approximately 6,000 m²
technical_specs.wave_height: Up to approximately 3.0 m at peak wave cycles in the Hurricane Wave Bay (described as China's largest indoor-style hurricane wave pool); typical recreational waves 0.3-1.5 m [uncertain on exact maximum]
technical_specs.wave_height_adjustability: Multi-mode wave generation cycling between calm, gentle rolling waves, and dramatic 'hurricane' surge waves on a programmed schedule; specific named tier levels not publicly published [uncertain]
technical_specs.wave_types: Recreational rolling waves and surge / hurricane waves for floating, bobbing, bodyboarding, and 'warrior surfing' (勇士冲浪) attraction; not designed for stand-up surfboard riding
technical_specs.waves_per_hour: Operates in cycles of active wave sets followed by rest intervals; specific waves-per-hour rate not publicly documented [uncertain]
technical_specs.wave_count_per_set: Set durations and per-set wave counts not publicly published; typical hurricane-mode cycles run several minutes of building swell followed by calm intervals [uncertain]
technical_specs.session_length: Continuous access during park operating hours; no per-session time limit on the wave pool [uncertain]
technical_specs.stance_rotation_schedule: N/A - not a surfing-focused pool; no Goofy/Regular rotation
technical_specs.wave_type_classification: Artificial recreational family wave pool with hurricane surge mode; snow-mountain themed; not a dedicated surf-shaped wave pool

pricing_sessions.price_range: Adult day ticket approximately RMB 208; child ticket approximately RMB 115; group / promotional pricing from RMB 50; combined Ocean Paradise + Water Kingdom family packages approximately RMB 577 for 2 adults + 1 child; night session (added in 2025) opens 18:00 [uncertain on current 2026 pricing]
pricing_sessions.session_skill_tiers: N/A - no skill-tiered surf sessions; single general admission grants access to wave pool and all 18 water attractions
pricing_sessions.equipment_rental: Life jackets typically provided free; lockers, towels, and swimwear available for rent on-site (typical RMB 30-80 deposits); the park does not provide free swimsuits and visitors must wear swimwear [uncertain on exact current fees]
pricing_sessions.coaching_fee: N/A - no surf coaching offered; the park is recreational family-oriented
pricing_sessions.coaching_packages: N/A - no surf coaching packages
pricing_sessions.membership_program: Annual passes and combo tickets bundling Ocean Paradise + Water Kingdom + on-island accommodation occasionally offered; specific Water-Kingdom-only annual pass pricing not publicly listed [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.corporate_team_building: Yes - the resort markets MICE, corporate retreats, and team-building packages via the world's largest convention center on the island; minimum group size and pricing handled case-by-case [uncertain on published rates]

experience_guide.best_season: Water Kingdom typically operates April-November with seasonal closures in winter; peak experience May-October when air and water temperatures are warmest; avoid Chinese national holidays (Spring Festival, Labor Day, National Day Golden Week, July-August summer break) for shorter queues
experience_guide.best_swell_window: N/A - artificial wave pool, not a natural surf spot
experience_guide.crowd_level: Generally moderate due to remote location on Danzhou's west coast; very high during Chinese public holidays and summer school break; relatively quiet on weekdays in shoulder season; user reviews note 'the queues are not very long' even during normal peak periods
experience_guide.booking_method: Online pre-purchase strongly recommended via OTAs; tickets also sold at the park gate; same-day reservation typically available
experience_guide.booking_platform: Ctrip (携程) / Trip.com, Meituan-Dianping (大众点评), Fliggy (飞猪), Qunar (去哪儿), Tuniu (途牛), Douyin (抖音), official Ocean Flower Island WeChat mini program
experience_guide.payment_options: Alipay, WeChat Pay, UnionPay, cash (RMB); international credit cards limited acceptance at park ticket counters but accepted at on-island international hotels [uncertain on current international card support at park gate]
experience_guide.skill_required: Zero - no surfing skill required; suitable for non-swimmers in shallow areas with life jackets and lifeguard supervision
experience_guide.age_eligibility: Children under 1.2 m height typically free or discounted; 1.2-1.5 m children's ticket; over 1.5 m adult ticket. Minors under 16 require adult supervision. Several thrill slides have minimum 1.2 m or 1.4 m height requirements; guests over 60 years or weighing over 90 kg are restricted from most rides [uncertain on exact wave-pool-specific limits]
experience_guide.child_friendly_score: 5 - explicitly designed as a snow-mountain themed family/children's water park with baby slides, zero-entry shallow areas, themed costumes, and dedicated kids' zones; co-located with the marine animal park makes a full family day out
experience_guide.sup_paddleboard_offering: Not offered as a structured program at the wave pool [uncertain]
experience_guide.photography_video_service: On-site professional photographers at major slides and wave pool; photo packages sold at exit kiosks; pricing varies seasonally [uncertain on exact current rates]
experience_guide.language_support: Mandarin primary; limited English support at ticket counters and rides; full multilingual service available at on-island international-brand hotels (some operating, others delayed) [uncertain on current staffing]
experience_guide.accessibility_for_disabled: General park pathways are wheelchair accessible; persons with valid disability ID receive discounted or free admission; no dedicated adaptive surfing program at the wave pool [uncertain on detailed wave-pool entry accessibility]
experience_guide.stance_preference_input: N/A - not a surf training pool
experience_guide.safety_notes: Lifeguards posted at all pools and water attractions per Chinese national waterpark safety standards; mandatory life jackets for non-swimmers and children; bag security screening at entry; pregnant women, those with heart conditions, recent surgery patients, intoxicated guests, and visitors over 60 or 90+ kg prohibited from most rides. Personal accident insurance bundled into ticket per Chinese theme park regulations
experience_guide.events_competitions_calendar: Hosts seasonal entertainment including 'Snow Mountain Night Show' (雪山夜秀) light performances added in 2025, Danzhou electronic music parties, water battles, and holiday-themed events; does not host competitive surfing events

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: Numerous Evergrande-developed apartment-style properties on Ocean Flower Island Islands 2 and 3, with rates RMB 400-1,500 per night via short-term-rental platforms; the broader Danzhou and Yangpu coastline have limited additional independent accommodation options, generally requiring 30-60 minute drive
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: Ocean Flower Island master-plan called for 58 hotels including a 7-star Peninsula-branded property and European-style castle hotel; many hotels were completed but operational status varies due to the Evergrande financial restructuring; currently operating on-island hotels include several mid-tier and apartment-style properties with rates from approximately RMB 500-2,500 per night [uncertain on specific operating hotel inventory in 2026]
surroundings.nearby_food: On-island: 12+ ocean-themed restaurants in the Children's World complex, 8 themed food streets, submarine-themed bar, and 6 commercial streets across the master plan (operational status of individual outlets varies); off-island: Yangpu Bay seafood villages and Baima Jing town (白马井镇) restaurants approximately 20-30 minutes drive
surroundings.transport: Self-drive (free island parking and bridge access from mainland), Didi/taxi from Danzhou, internal monorail and inter-island shuttle on Ocean Flower Island, hotel shuttle services from Haikou and Sanya offered by some on-island hotels
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: Approximately 130 km / 1.5-2 hours drive from Haikou Meilan International Airport (HAK); approximately 250 km / 3 hours from Sanya Phoenix International Airport (SYX); nearest high-speed rail station is Baima Jing (白马井站) on the Hainan Western Ring High-Speed Railway, approximately 15 km / 20 minutes drive with airport/HSR shuttle services available
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Combine Ocean Flower Island Water Kingdom + Children's World Ocean Paradise + Snow Mountain Children's World theme park (multi-day pass); pair with Yangpu Bay mangrove ecological park, Dongpo Academy (东坡书院) Su Shi historical site in Danzhou, and Lan Yang Bay (兰洋温泉) hot springs; for the broader Hainan west coast loop, extend to Linchang (临场) salt fields and the Stone Flower Cave (石花水洞) geological park

pros: Massive 6,000 m² 'Hurricane Wave Bay' wave pool capable of generating ~3 m waves - one of the largest in Hainan
Unique snow-mountain themed design - the only such concept in South China
Co-located Children's World Ocean Paradise marine park makes a full multi-day family destination
WTI-designed with internationally award-winning slides from ProSlide and WhiteWater West
Generally less crowded than Atlantis Sanya with shorter queues even in peak periods
cons: Remote west-coast location far from Sanya tourism cluster - long drive or HSR transfer required
Operating status and visitor experience affected by ongoing Evergrande Group restructuring; some on-island hotels and amenities remain unfinished or non-operational
Seasonal water-park closures in winter months
Limited international F&B and hospitality services compared to Sanya destinations
Past controversy over illegal-construction demolition orders (2021-2022) and 'Ugliest Buildings in China' ranking has affected overall island reputation [uncertain on current resolution status]
reviews_reputation.user_reviews: Ctrip (携程) reviews praise the wave pool and unique snow-mountain theming, with multiple positive notes about minimal queues; Dianping (大众点评) reviews highlight the value-for-money family packages and friendly staff; Xiaohongshu (小红书) extensive coverage of the photogenic snow-mountain backdrop and ferris wheel; some reviews caution about heat exposure and the long travel distance from Haikou or Sanya [uncertain on exact current scores]
reviews_reputation.recommended_for: Families with children, road-trippers exploring Hainan's west coast, multi-generational travelers, photo/social-media visitors seeking unique snow-mountain backdrop, budget-conscious families avoiding Sanya pricing; NOT recommended for serious surfers seeking training waves or short-trip visitors based in Sanya
reviews_reputation.operator_brand_partnership: Originally developed by Evergrande Group (恒大集团) under its Children's World tourism division; current operator is Hengda Children's World Group via Danzhou Changyu Tourism Development Co., Ltd.; designed by WTI (WhiteWater Tube & Inflatables) Design and US firm IdeAttack (艾迪泰克) with PGAV; slides and equipment by ProSlide, WhiteWater West, Guangdong H-Fun, and Dalang

basic_info.name: Qingshui Bay (Clearwater Bay) / 陵水·清水灣
basic_info.location: Yingzhou Town and Xinchun Town, Lingshui Li Autonomous County, Hainan Province, China; ~4.8 km from the Yingzhou exit of the Hainan East Ring Expressway, ~12 km coastline at 18° N latitude on the southeast coast
basic_info.operator: Qingshui Bay Tourist Resort District (清水灣旅遊度假區) - Lingshui County government-managed scenic zone; coastline shared by multiple resort developers (Country Garden 碧桂園, Agile 雅居樂, Greentown 綠城, R&F 富力, Westin / Kimpton hotels) and surf clubs (SHAKA SURF Qingshui Bay branch, Chasing Waves, Huanyang Surf Club, Hang5 etc.)
basic_info.opening_date: N/A (natural surf spot; tourist resort district designated since the 2000s)

technical_specs.wave_technology_brand: N/A (natural waves)
technical_specs.pool_size: Approximately 12 km of south-facing coastline with three identifiable surf zones: Agile Sales-Office Beach (雅居樂售樓處沙灘), Greentown Lanwan Town Commercial Street Beach (綠城藍灣小鎮商業街門前沙灘), and Coral Palace Beach (珊瑚宮殿) - the last being a reef break
technical_specs.wave_height: Seasonal average 0.5-1.5 m; summer SE/S swells (Jun-Sep) commonly 0.8-1.5 m and can exceed 1 m during typhoon swells; winter NE swells (Nov-Mar) sheltered by Lanwan/Westin headlands deliver more stable ~1 m waves [uncertain]
technical_specs.wave_height_adjustability: Not adjustable (natural ocean swell driven by SE summer monsoon, NE winter monsoon, and typhoon groundswell)
technical_specs.wave_types: Mostly mellow beach break with sandy bottom (beginner-friendly at Agile and Greentown sections); plus one reef break at Coral Palace for intermediate/advanced surfers; gentle south-facing geometry filters out the largest winter NE swells
technical_specs.waves_per_hour: N/A (natural surf spot)
technical_specs.wave_count_per_set: N/A (natural ocean sets, typically 3-6 waves per set with 8-12 second intervals depending on swell period) [uncertain]
technical_specs.session_length: N/A (open-ocean access; typical surf school lessons are ~90-120 minutes)
technical_specs.stance_rotation_schedule: N/A (natural beach break; works for both Goofy and Regular stance depending on peak)
technical_specs.wave_type_classification: Predominantly beach break (south-facing) with one reef section; suits soft-tops and longboards in summer, mid-length / shortboard in winter

pricing_sessions.price_range: Free open-ocean access at the public beach; surf school lessons typically 380-680 RMB for a 90-120 minute group/private session including board, leash and rashguard [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.session_skill_tiers: Tiered by individual schools: 1-on-4 group beginner experience ~398-498 RMB; 1-on-2 intermediate ~580-780 RMB; 1-on-1 private ~680-1,200 RMB; star-rating certification courses (1-star, 2-star) available at SHAKA, Chasing Waves and Huanyang [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.equipment_rental: Soft-top board ~80-120 RMB/session (with refundable deposit ~300-500 RMB) or 150-300 RMB/day for performance shortboards; rashguard ~30-50 RMB; leash typically included [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.coaching_fee: Group lesson 200-400 RMB per person; private 1-on-1 lesson 600-1,000 RMB for ~2 hours; multi-day camps offered by SHAKA SURF and Chasing Waves [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.coaching_packages: Multi-day surf-and-stay camps 1,500-3,500 RMB at Chasing Waves and SHAKA; combined surf + free-diving packages at Chasing Waves; photo/video bundles 100-300 RMB add-on [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.membership_program: Most schools sell 5-/10-session punch cards 1,800-3,500 RMB; no formal bay-wide membership program [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.corporate_team_building: Yes - SHAKA SURF, Chasing Waves and Huanyang accept corporate team-building bookings; minimum group sizes typically 8-15 people; pricing on request [uncertain]

experience_guide.best_season: Year-round surfable with two distinct windows: June-September for the best summer SE/S swells (often better than Wanning), October-February for gentler beach break suitable for beginners
experience_guide.best_swell_window: June-September: SE/S typhoon-period swells 0.8-1.5 m, period 6-10 s, offshore N winds in early morning; secondary winter NE swell window October-March produces smaller, sheltered beginner waves
experience_guide.crowd_level: Light to moderate crowd compared to Riyue Bay; busiest during Spring Festival, May Day and National Day holidays and during the Qingshui Bay Resort Season (Feb 1-28); weekday mornings nearly empty
experience_guide.booking_method: Walk-in at any beachfront surf club; online booking via Dianping (大眾點評), Meituan (美團), Ctrip (攜程), Qunar (去哪兒) and Klook; WeChat mini-programs of individual clubs (SHAKA, Chasing Waves, Huanyang)
experience_guide.booking_platform: Dianping (大眾點評), Meituan (美團), Ctrip / Trip.com (攜程), Qunar (去哪兒), Xiaohongshu (小紅書), WeChat mini-programs
experience_guide.payment_options: WeChat Pay and Alipay universally accepted; cash accepted at most clubs and rentals; international credit cards generally only accepted at Westin / Kimpton / Country Garden hotels and not at independent surf shops
experience_guide.skill_required: All levels - sandy beach break sections suit zero-base beginners; reef section at Coral Palace requires intermediate paddling and pop-up; summer typhoon swell windows for advanced
experience_guide.age_eligibility: Most surf schools accept ages 6+ for kids' lessons; no formal upper age limit; participants must be able to swim
experience_guide.child_friendly_score: 4 out of 5 - shallow gradient (wade 200 m before 2 m depth), Class I water quality with 25 m visibility, gentle sand-bottom waves, kids' soft-tops at major schools, plus child-friendly Westin / Kimpton resort facilities; no enclosed kids' surf pool
experience_guide.sup_paddleboard_offering: Yes - SUP rentals and lessons available at Chasing Waves, Huanyang Surf Club and several beach operators; calm summer mornings and the long flat-water shoreline are ideal for SUP
experience_guide.photography_video_service: Photo / video service offered by most clubs; typical pricing 100-300 RMB for session photos, 300-580 RMB for edited video; some private classes include GoPro/SLR photos free [uncertain]
experience_guide.language_support: Limited English at most local clubs; Chasing Waves and SHAKA SURF have some English-speaking instructors; Mandarin dominant at the bay
experience_guide.accessibility_for_disabled: No formal adaptive surfing program; the wide hard-sand beach allows beach wheelchair access at the Westin and Kimpton resort frontages [uncertain]
experience_guide.stance_preference_input: Not required when booking; stance does not affect access (south-facing beach break works peaks both directions)
experience_guide.safety_notes: Generally safer than Riyue Bay due to sheltered south-facing geometry and gentle gradient; lifeguards present at major resort beach sections only; ISA-certified coaches at SHAKA SURF and Chasing Waves; surf insurance recommended; rip currents possible during typhoon swells
experience_guide.events_competitions_calendar: Annual Qingshui Bay Resort Season and International Yacht Festival (清水灣度假季暨國際遊艇節) Feb 1-28 each year; not a regular host of WSL/ISA surf competitions (those are at Riyue Bay); included in 2026 Hainan official 'Island Water-Sports Adventure Route' (玩轉海島動感之旅 3D2N) and 'Island-Discovery Ocean Immersion Route' (環島發現親海之旅 10D9N)

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: Wide range from beachfront 5-star to mid-range: Westin Shimei Bay, Kimpton Qingshui Bay (opened May 2025, 289 rooms), Country Garden Holiday Hotel, Greentown Lanwan Town Hotel, Agile Resort - typical rates 800-3,000 RMB; budget guesthouses in Yingzhou Town 200-500 RMB/night
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: Multiple resort hotels integrated into the Qingshui Bay Tourist Resort District itself, including Kimpton Qingshui Bay (289 rooms, opened May 2025), Westin Shimei Bay (adjacent), Sheraton, Country Garden Holiday Hotel, Royal Tulip and Agile/Greentown branded properties; rates from ~700 to 3,000+ RMB [uncertain on exact 2026 inventory]
surroundings.nearby_food: Resort-hotel restaurants (Cantonese, Hainanese, Western buffet, all-day dining), Greentown Lanwan Town Commercial Street cafes and seafood, Yingzhou Town local Hainanese seafood and Wenchang chicken stalls, Danjia (疍家) fishing-raft seafood at Xincun Port; per-person spend 50-300 RMB
surroundings.transport: Self-drive via Hainan East Ring Expressway G98 (Yingzhou exit), high-speed train to Lingshui Station (~30 min by taxi/Didi), free or paid resort shuttles from Sanya, Didi/taxi widely available
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: ~1 hour / ~70 km drive from Sanya Phoenix International Airport (SYX) via G98 East Ring Expressway; ~3 hours / ~220 km from Haikou Meilan International Airport (HAK); high-speed rail to Lingshui Station ~30-40 min from the bay
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Qingshui Bay surf/SUP + Fenjiezhou Island (分界洲島) diving + Nanwan Monkey Island (南灣猴島) + Xincun Port Danjia floating fishing village + Hongjiaoling paragliding base + Xiangshui Bay (香水灣) and Fuli Bay (富力灣) combined as a 2-3 day Lingshui surf-and-island itinerary; can be linked north to Wanning Riyue Bay for a full Hainan surf trip

pros: World-class water quality (Class I marine standard, 25 m visibility) and 'singing sand' beach (one of three globally with Hawaii and Australia's Gold Coast)
Sheltered south-facing 12 km arc with gentle gradient - among Hainan's most beginner-friendly natural surf settings
Closer to Sanya Phoenix Airport (~1 hour) than Riyue Bay (~1.5 hours), much easier day-trip access
Less crowded than Wanning's surf cluster; uncrowded peaks at Coral Palace reef in summer
Strong summer (Jun-Sep) swell window - reportedly outperforms Wanning during peak typhoon-period swells
cons: Winter NE swell is partially blocked by hotel headlands - serious surfers still prefer Riyue Bay in Dec-Feb
Surf infrastructure smaller than Riyue Bay (fewer than ~10 surf clubs vs 50+ at Riyue Bay)
Limited English support outside flagship Westin/Kimpton hotels
Resort area is spread out - walking between surf zones can be inconvenient without a car or e-bike
Public beach access points can be confusing because most frontage is gated by branded resorts
reviews_reputation.user_reviews: Trip.com / Tripadvisor: 4.5+/5 average for the bay as a destination; Xiaohongshu (小紅書) features Qingshui Bay as a 'healing' beach destination with strong ratings for water clarity and family travel; Ctrip surf-club reviews for SHAKA SURF Qingshui Bay branch show 4.7+/5 with praise for patient instructors
reviews_reputation.recommended_for: Beginner to intermediate surfers (especially in summer); families with kids; couples and photography travelers; SUP enthusiasts; resort-style surf trips; visitors combining surf + Fenjiezhou diving + Lingshui cultural experiences
reviews_reputation.operator_brand_partnership: Qingshui Bay Tourist Resort District is government-managed by Lingshui County; resort developers include Country Garden 碧桂園, Agile 雅居樂, Greentown 綠城 and R&F 富力; 2026 Hainan Marine Tourism Year designated Qingshui Bay as a key node on the official 'Island Water-Sports Adventure Route' (玩轉海島動感之旅) curated by Hainan Provincial Tourism Department

basic_info.name: Queen's Bay (Houhai) / 三亞·後海皇后灣 (皇后湾)
basic_info.location: Queen's Bay, southern end of Haitang Bay (国家海岸海棠湾南端), Tenghai (Houhai) Village, Haitang District, Sanya City, Hainan Province; ~30 km from downtown Sanya, between Wuzhizhou Island ferry pier and Yalong Bay; the named cove inside Houhai Bay considered the principal beginner surf zone
basic_info.operator: Sanya Houhai Tourism Development Company (三亚后海旅游发展公司, established 2023) administers and standardizes the bay; 80+ independent surf clubs and 50+ guesthouses operate within Houhai Village; pioneer ISA-certified instructors include Yang Xingfu (杨兴福); supervised by Haitang District Government and Sanya Tourism Board
basic_info.opening_date: N/A (natural surf spot; commercial surfing scene developed since approximately 2008, accelerated 2018-2024)

technical_specs.wave_technology_brand: N/A (natural waves)
technical_specs.pool_size: Crescent-shaped sheltered cove forming the southern arc of Houhai Bay; main beginner zone roughly 500-800 m of beach with shallow apron extending ~50 m before water reaches knee-depth [uncertain]
technical_specs.wave_height: Year-round average ~1.0 m; winter (Oct-Apr) 1.0-1.5 m steady, summer (May-Sep) often only 0.3-0.5 m soft mush; typhoon swells (Aug-Sep) can briefly bring 1.5-2.5 m days when beach may close
technical_specs.wave_height_adjustability: Not adjustable (natural ocean swell); cove geometry naturally damps swell so waves remain small/gentle even on bigger outside days; different positions inside the arc produce different wave sizes (inside for beginners, slightly outside for intermediates)
technical_specs.wave_types: Beach break over sand with both left and right peelers; predominantly soft, mellow whitewater and shoulder waves suited to longboard cruising; rated among the world's ten safest surf zones for beginners; rare bigger swells offer small intermediate face waves
technical_specs.waves_per_hour: N/A (natural surf spot)
technical_specs.wave_count_per_set: N/A (natural ocean sets, typically 2-4 waves per set with 6-10 second intervals) [uncertain]
technical_specs.session_length: N/A (open ocean access; guided surf-school lessons typically 120 minutes)
technical_specs.stance_rotation_schedule: N/A (beach break offers both lefts and rights simultaneously; no daily rotation)
technical_specs.wave_type_classification: Beach break over sand inside a sheltered crescent cove; ideal for soft-top longboards and beginner foamies; light, steady swell year-round

pricing_sessions.price_range: Free open-ocean access; entry-level taster lessons widely advertised at ~100-300 RMB; standard 2-hour beginner lesson 300-580 RMB including board and rashguard; private 1-on-1 ~500 RMB
pricing_sessions.session_skill_tiers: Taster experience (~100 RMB shore-time entry); standard beginner group lesson 2 hours ~300-400 RMB; experience class 580 RMB/person at premium clubs (e.g., Wanmei Dahai 完美大海); zero-base private 500 RMB/session at Yinli Surf (引力冲浪) or Jile (亼楽) club; intermediate coaching by appointment [uncertain pricing for advanced]
pricing_sessions.equipment_rental: Soft-top board ~150 RMB/day (commonly until 7 PM); rashguard usually included; leash and zinc included; SUP rental ~150-200 RMB/day; wetsuits seldom needed (water 24-29C)
pricing_sessions.coaching_fee: Group lesson 200-400 RMB per person (2 hours); private 1-on-1 lesson 500 RMB at Jile/Yinli; package often bundles board until 7 PM, rashguard, GoPro footage and 1-2 free photos
pricing_sessions.coaching_packages: Day pass (lesson + board until 7 PM + rashguard + GoPro footage) ~300-400 RMB; multi-day camps 1,500-3,500 RMB; couple/family packages negotiable; chain clubs offer photo + lesson combos around 580 RMB [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.membership_program: Many clubs sell 10-session punch cards ~1,500-2,500 RMB; informal monthly memberships at popular venues; no bay-wide membership system [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.corporate_team_building: Most large clubs accept team-building groups; common minimum 10-20 people; pricing 200-400 RMB per person plus group catering; some bayside guesthouses offer all-inclusive corporate packages [uncertain]

experience_guide.best_season: Year-round surfable; best gentle waves April-October for absolute beginners; cleanest most consistent waves October-April (NE monsoon); avoid typhoon-warning days Aug-Sep when bay may close
experience_guide.best_swell_window: October-April (NE monsoon) brings clean ~1.0-1.5 m days; summer SE/S swells provide playful 0.3-0.5 m mush; typhoon groundswells Aug-Oct may deliver 1.5-2.5 m days (often closed for safety)
experience_guide.crowd_level: Very crowded in peak winter (Nov-Feb) and Chinese Spring Festival/National Day; hundreds of beginners simultaneously in afternoons; weekday early mornings (6-8 AM) least crowded; afternoon party-wave atmosphere because surf etiquette still developing
experience_guide.booking_method: Walk-in at any beachfront surf club; online via Dianping, Meituan, Xiaohongshu, club WeChat mini-programs, Trip.com/Ctrip, Klook (Hainan Houhai Surfing Experience listings); resort concierge bookings via Rosewood Sanya, Atlantis Sanya, Edition
experience_guide.booking_platform: Dianping (大众点评), Meituan (美团), Xiaohongshu (小红书), Trip.com / Ctrip (携程), Klook, Qunar, WeChat club mini-programs
experience_guide.payment_options: WeChat Pay and Alipay universally accepted; cash accepted; international credit cards rarely accepted at village-level shops (only at adjacent 5-star resorts)
experience_guide.skill_required: Zero-base beginners welcome and form vast majority of users; intermediate longboarders enjoy small-day cruising; not suitable for advanced surfers seeking power
experience_guide.age_eligibility: Most schools accept kids 6+ with parental supervision; no formal upper age limit; participants must be able to swim or wear flotation; pregnant women generally restricted
experience_guide.child_friendly_score: 5/5 - shallow sandy bay where you can walk 50 m and water still only reaches knee-depth; extremely gentle waves; many schools offer dedicated kids' lessons and soft-top boards; family-favorite among Chinese parents
experience_guide.sup_paddleboard_offering: Yes - SUP rentals (~150-200 RMB/day) and lessons widely available at most clubs; kite-surf and skateboard demos also offered
experience_guide.photography_video_service: Most lesson packages include free GoPro session footage and 1-3 photos; standalone photo set 100-300 RMB; professional drone shoots 300-500 RMB; higher-end edited video packages 200-580 RMB [uncertain]
experience_guide.language_support: Limited English; some foreigner-frequented shops have English-speaking coaches; Mandarin dominant; Russian common at certain shops popular with Russian visitors
experience_guide.accessibility_for_disabled: No formal adaptive surfing program; sandy beach access without paved boardwalks; some surf hostels have ground-floor rooms but not certified accessible [uncertain]
experience_guide.stance_preference_input: Not required when booking; cove offers both lefts and rights so stance is irrelevant
experience_guide.safety_notes: Generally very safe shallow sandy bottom with no reef hazards; main risks are crowding (board collisions), strong sun and heat, and typhoon-period closures; lifeguards on main beach during peak hours; most schools include 5 RMB insurance; ISA-certified coaches at top clubs (Yang Xingfu Surf Club and others); Sanya Houhai Tourism Development Company implementing standardized safety regulations since 2023
experience_guide.events_competitions_calendar: Hosts smaller-scale Hainan Provincial qualifiers, surf festivals, and brand demo days (Vans, Quiksilver, Roxy); not the venue for major WSL/ISA tour stops (those go to Riyue Bay); annual surf-village beach parties and summer fishing-village festivals [uncertain exact calendar]

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: 50+ surf-themed guesthouses and hostels in Houhai Village ranging 80-300 RMB (hostel dorms) to 300-700 RMB (private rooms); Jile Surf Inn (亼楽) themed lodge with on-site board display and cafe; Elaine Seaview Surf Design Guesthouse from ~85 RMB; Yunhai Jingqi Surf Hostel from ~210 RMB; one-night average ~340 RMB
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: No single integrated resort directly on Queen's Bay sand; surf-club operated lodges include Jile Surf-themed Inn, Houhai Surfing Sea View Resort Homestay, Homecoming Seascape Surfing Vacation; nearby 5-star options on Haitang Bay shoreline include Rosewood Sanya, Atlantis Sanya, Edition Sanya offering surf concierge transfers (~few km) [uncertain pricing details]
surroundings.nearby_food: Tanka seafood (yellow chili and sour orange dip), Hainanese qingbuliang (清補涼), baoluo noodles (抱罗粉), street BBQ, fresh coconut, SASA de Sea local cuisine, Surf House Cafe, Jile Cafe, fishing-village night market with daily fresh-catch sales; per-person spend 50-150 RMB
surroundings.transport: Self-drive ~40-60 minutes from Sanya downtown; Didi/taxi widely available (~140 RMB from airport); Sanya bus line 28 reaches Wuzhizhou Island stop + 10-min walk; high-speed rail to Yalongwan Station then Didi ~30 RMB
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: ~50 km / ~60 minutes drive from Sanya Phoenix International Airport (SYX) (~140 RMB taxi); ~30 minutes from Sanya central rail station; ~10 minutes drive from Wuzhizhou Island ferry; high-speed rail to Yalongwan Station + 30 min Didi
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Queen's Bay surfing + Wuzhizhou Island (蜈支洲岛) snorkeling/dive + Haitang Bay duty-free shopping + Yalong Bay beach + Atlantis Sanya/Edition Sanya resort day; classic 3-5 day Sanya beach + surf + family itinerary

pros: Rated among the world's ten safest surf zones; ideal for absolute first-timers and children
Shallow sandy apron lets you walk far before water reaches knee height - perfect for kids and non-swimmers
Year-round surfable conditions (~200+ days/year) with consistent gentle waves
Extreme density of surf clubs (80+ in adjacent Houhai Village) makes walk-in lessons easy and prices competitive
More relaxed and 'romantic' bay atmosphere than commercial Yalong Bay; coral-rich seabed and white sand
cons: Extremely crowded in peak season; party-wave congestion and inconsistent surf etiquette in lineup
Waves too small/soft for intermediate-advanced surfers seeking power or barrels
Surf school quality varies widely; many quickly-certified coaches with limited teaching experience
Increasing commercialization eroding original fishing-village charm
Limited English; foreign visitors must rely on translation apps; international cards seldom accepted
reviews_reputation.user_reviews: Trip.com / Tripadvisor: 4.0-4.5/5; Xiaohongshu features over 520,000 mentions of 'Houhai' surfing tagged content with thousands of viral 'Houhai surf girl' posts; Dianping reviews praise vibe and accessibility but flag crowding and inconsistent coaching quality; Mafengwo describes Queen's Bay as 'more atmospheric than Yalong Bay'; Baidu Baike notes 80+ registered clubs as of 2024
reviews_reputation.recommended_for: Absolute beginners and first-time surfers; family/parent-child trips; children ages 6-12; couples seeking surf + nightlife combo; social-media/photo-focused travellers; longboarders; surf-vacation groups; budget travellers
reviews_reputation.operator_brand_partnership: Sanya Houhai Tourism Development Company established 2023 standardizes operations; Sanya Tourism Board promotes Houhai/Queen's Bay as official surf village destination; pioneer school Yang Xingfu Surf Club ISA-affiliated; brand demos by Vans, Quiksilver, Roxy at periodic events; nearby Rosewood Sanya offers private surf lessons via talent coaches at Houhai [uncertain partnerships]

basic_info.name: Hainan R&F Ocean Paradise / R&F Water World (海南富力海洋欢乐世界 / 富力水世界)
basic_info.location: No. 1 Shuanghu Avenue, Li'an Town, Lingshui Li Autonomous County, Hainan Province, China (海南省陵水黎族自治县黎安镇双湖大道1号)
basic_info.operator: R&F Properties (富力地产) - cultural tourism division; on-site Hyatt Regency Hainan Ocean Paradise Resort operated by Hyatt Hotels Corporation

technical_specs.wave_technology_brand: Not publicly disclosed; family-recreational wave generation system (likely WhiteWater West or comparable Chinese OEM) [uncertain]
technical_specs.pool_size: Wave pool over 4,000 m²; entire R&F Water World occupies a portion of the broader 870-acre park footprint
technical_specs.wave_height: Up to approximately 1.5 m at peak wave cycles (recreational family waves, not surf-grade) [uncertain]
technical_specs.wave_height_adjustability: Wave intensity cycles between calm and peak (~1.5 m) on a programmed schedule; specific named tier levels not publicly published [uncertain]
technical_specs.wave_types: Recreational rolling/surge waves for floating, bobbing, and bodyboarding in the central wave bay; not designed for stand-up surfing or barrel waves
technical_specs.waves_per_hour: Operates in cycles of active wave sets followed by rest intervals; specific waves-per-hour rate not publicly documented [uncertain]
technical_specs.wave_count_per_set: Set durations and per-set wave counts not publicly published; typical family wave pool cycles run several minutes of waves followed by calm intervals [uncertain]
technical_specs.session_length: Continuous access during park operating hours; no per-session time limit on the wave pool [uncertain]
technical_specs.stance_rotation_schedule: N/A - not a surfing-focused pool; no Goofy/Regular rotation
technical_specs.wave_type_classification: Artificial recreational family wave pool; not a dedicated surf-shaped wave pool

pricing_sessions.price_range: R&F Water World adult day ticket approximately RMB 178 (promotional) to RMB 268 (peak); combined Ocean Paradise + Water World adult day ticket approximately RMB 278-368; child / senior / military discount tickets available; free for hotel guests on selected packages [uncertain on current 2026 pricing]
pricing_sessions.session_skill_tiers: N/A - no skill-tiered surf sessions; single general admission grants access to wave pool and all 16 water rides
pricing_sessions.equipment_rental: Life jackets provided free; lockers, towels, swimwear, and bodyboards available for rent on-site (typical RMB 30-80 deposits) [uncertain on exact current fees]
pricing_sessions.coaching_fee: N/A - no surf coaching offered; the park is recreational family-oriented
pricing_sessions.coaching_packages: N/A - no surf coaching packages
pricing_sessions.membership_program: R&F resort offers annual passes and combo tickets bundling Ocean Paradise + Water World + Hyatt Regency stays; specific Water-World-only annual pass pricing not publicly listed [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.corporate_team_building: Yes - the resort actively markets MICE and team-building packages through Hyatt Regency and R&F resort sales; minimum group size and pricing handled case-by-case [uncertain on published rates]

experience_guide.best_season: Water World typically operates seasonally April-October when air and sea temperatures are warmest; the broader Ocean Paradise marine park operates year-round. Avoid Chinese national holidays (Spring Festival, Labor Day, National Day Golden Week, July-August summer break) for shorter queues
experience_guide.best_swell_window: N/A - artificial wave pool, not a natural surf spot
experience_guide.crowd_level: Very high during Chinese public holidays and summer school break; moderate on weekends; relatively quiet on weekdays in shoulder season (April, May, September, October). Newer park with less name-recognition than Atlantis Sanya so generally less crowded than Haitang Bay competitors
experience_guide.booking_method: Online pre-purchase strongly recommended via OTAs and the park's official WeChat mini program; tickets also sold at the park gate
experience_guide.booking_platform: Ctrip (携程) / Trip.com, Meituan-Dianping (大众点评), Fliggy (飞猪), Qunar (去哪儿), Douyin (抖音), official R&F Ocean Paradise WeChat mini program (海南海洋欢乐世界 official account)
experience_guide.payment_options: Alipay, WeChat Pay, UnionPay, cash (RMB); international credit cards accepted at the on-site Hyatt Regency hotel but limited acceptance at park ticket counters [uncertain on current international card support at park gate]
experience_guide.skill_required: Zero - no surfing skill required; suitable for non-swimmers in shallow areas with life jackets and lifeguard supervision
experience_guide.age_eligibility: Children under 1.0 m height free; 1.0 m to 1.4 m children's ticket; over 1.4 m adult ticket. Children under 16 require adult supervision. Some thrill water slides have minimum 1.2 m or 1.4 m height requirements; guests over 60 years or weighing over 90 kg may be restricted from select rides [uncertain on exact wave-pool-specific limits]
experience_guide.child_friendly_score: 5 - explicitly marketed as a family/parent-child destination; large kids' play zone, baby slide ('宝贝溜溜滑'), zero-entry shallow areas, free life jackets, and lifeguards; co-located with the marine animal park makes a full family day out
experience_guide.sup_paddleboard_offering: Not offered as a structured program at the wave pool [uncertain]
experience_guide.photography_video_service: On-site professional photographers at major slides and wave pool; photo packages sold at exit kiosks; pricing varies seasonally [uncertain on exact current rates]
experience_guide.language_support: Mandarin primary; limited English support at ticket counters; full multilingual service (English / Russian / Cantonese) at the on-site Hyatt Regency hotel front desk and concierge
experience_guide.accessibility_for_disabled: General park pathways are wheelchair accessible; persons with valid disability ID receive discounted or free admission; no dedicated adaptive surfing program at the wave pool [uncertain on detailed wave-pool entry accessibility]
experience_guide.stance_preference_input: N/A - not a surf training pool
experience_guide.safety_notes: Lifeguards posted at all pools and water attractions per Chinese national waterpark safety standards; mandatory life jackets for non-swimmers and children; pre-set wave cycle warnings broadcast before each set; pregnant women, those with heart conditions, recent surgery patients, and intoxicated guests prohibited from the wave pool. Personal accident insurance bundled into ticket per Chinese theme park regulations
experience_guide.events_competitions_calendar: Hosts seasonal entertainment shows (DJ pool parties, electronic music events, holiday-themed performances) at the wave pool; does not host competitive surfing events

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: Multiple R&F-developed properties around the resort; nearby Li'an Lagoon and Qingshui Bay (Clearwater Bay) hotels including The Sanya EDITION, Mingshen Goldenpalm Resort, Nantian Hot Springs Resort, with rates RMB 800-3,500+ per night
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: Hyatt Regency Hainan Ocean Paradise Resort - approximately 1,000 rooms (the first 1,000-room Hyatt Regency in Greater China); rates from approximately RMB 1,200-3,500 per night including breakfast; multiple restaurants, three swimming pools, kids club, and direct waterpark access; resort-stay packages bundle Ocean Paradise tickets
surroundings.nearby_food: On-site: 2 themed restaurants inside R&F Water World plus multiple F&B outlets within Ocean Paradise marine park; Hyatt Regency offers all-day dining, Cantonese, Italian, and pool-bar options. Off-site: Li'an Town (黎安镇) seafood restaurants, Xiangshui Bay (香水湾) and Qingshui Bay (清水湾) resort dining clusters approximately 15-30 minutes by car
surroundings.transport: Self-drive (free resort parking), Didi/taxi from Lingshui or Sanya, Lingshui public bus route 91 to 'Ocean Park' (海洋公园) stop, Hyatt Regency shuttle from Sanya Phoenix International Airport (paid)
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: Approximately 80 km / 60-75 minutes drive from Sanya Phoenix International Airport (SYX); approximately 200 km / 2.5-3 hours drive from Haikou Meilan International Airport (HAK); nearest high-speed rail station is Lingshui (陵水站) on the Hainan East Ring High-Speed Railway, approximately 30 km / 30 minutes drive
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Pair with Qingshui Bay (清水湾) beach + Fenjiezhou Island (分界洲岛) day trip; combine with Boundary Island diving and Sanya East Coast tour; multi-day Hyatt Regency stay packages bundle Ocean Paradise + Water World + South China Sea Train monorail; can also extend to Wanning Riyue Bay (日月湾) for natural-wave surfing approximately 50 km north

pros: Newer integrated marine park + waterpark + Hyatt Regency hotel - one-stop family destination
Less crowded than Atlantis Sanya; better photo spots and shorter queues
Large 4,000+ m² wave pool with capacity for 2,000+ guests
Co-located 1,000-room Hyatt Regency offers high-quality on-site stay with direct park access
Family-friendly with strong child appeal: marine animals, dolphins, polar exhibits, plus the water park
cons: Water World operates seasonally (typically closed in winter months) while marine park stays open year-round
Less mature F&B and retail compared to Atlantis Sanya
Wave intensity is mild and family-oriented - not suitable for serious surfers or skill development
Located further from Sanya city center (75+ minutes drive); requires planning for transport
R&F Group financial restructuring concerns have raised occasional uncertainty about long-term operations [uncertain on current financial status]
reviews_reputation.user_reviews: Ctrip (携程) average rating approximately 4.5/5; Dianping (大众点评) reviews praise the marine animal exhibits and wave pool but note seasonal water-park closures and mid-day heat; Xiaohongshu (小红书) extensive positive coverage highlighting photogenic ferris wheel and dolphin shows; Mafengwo (马蜂窝) reviews recommend bundling with Hyatt Regency stay for best value [uncertain on exact current scores]
reviews_reputation.recommended_for: Families with children, multi-generational travelers, leisure tourists, photo/social-media visitors, hotel guests at Hyatt Regency, corporate group buyouts; NOT recommended for serious surfers seeking training waves
reviews_reputation.operator_brand_partnership: Owned and developed by R&F Properties (富力地产) cultural tourism division; on-site hotel operated under Hyatt Regency brand by Hyatt Hotels Corporation; marine animal program developed in partnership with international aquarium consultants; AAAA-level national tourist attraction designation awarded October 2022
