basic_info.name: Riyue Bay (Sun and Moon Bay) / 萬寧日月灣
basic_info.location: Riyue Bay, Hexin Village (Tianxin Cun area), Wanning City, Hainan Province, China; ~87 km north of Sanya, ~168 km south of Haikou
basic_info.operator: China National Surfing Training Base; surf cluster around Tianxin Village (Hexin) operated by 50+ independent surf clubs and guesthouses; new adjacent PerfectSwell Surf Resort operated by China Tourism Group (CTG) / AWM venue partnership opened Nov 11, 2025
basic_info.opening_date: N/A (natural surf spot; recognized as national surf training base since 2010s)

technical_specs.wave_technology_brand: N/A (natural waves)
technical_specs.pool_size: Bay extends ~5-7 km of coastline with multiple breaks (Beachie, Main Point, Ghost Hotel) and roughly 15 left point setups along the NE-E facing shoreline [uncertain]
technical_specs.wave_height: Seasonal average 0.6-2.0 m; winter peaks (Oct-Mar) regularly produce 2 m sets, summer/monsoon waves typically 0.5-1.2 m
technical_specs.wave_height_adjustability: Not adjustable (natural ocean swell driven by NE monsoon and typhoon groundswell)
technical_specs.wave_types: Mix of point break (predominantly left-handers at Main Point and Ghost Hotel) and beach break (Beachie zone for beginners); long peeling lefts suit longboard and shortboard intermediate-to-advanced surfers
technical_specs.waves_per_hour: N/A (natural surf spot)
technical_specs.wave_count_per_set: N/A (natural ocean sets, typically 3-6 waves per set with 8-15 second intervals depending on swell period) [uncertain]
technical_specs.session_length: N/A (open ocean access; surf school lessons typically 90-120 minutes)
technical_specs.stance_rotation_schedule: N/A (natural left-hand point breaks favor goofy-foot frontside; regulars surf backside)
technical_specs.wave_type_classification: Predominantly point break (left-handers) with adjacent beach break sections; suits longboards in summer and shortboards/funboards in winter

pricing_sessions.price_range: Free open-ocean access; surf school lessons typically 300-500 RMB for 2-hour group session including board and rashguard; private lessons 500-800 RMB
pricing_sessions.session_skill_tiers: Tiered by surf school: Beginner group lesson ~300 RMB; Intermediate coaching ~500 RMB; Advanced/private 600-800 RMB; competition-level coaching by appointment [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.equipment_rental: Soft-top board ~80-100 RMB/session (with ~500 RMB deposit) or 150-300 RMB/day for performance shortboards; rashguard ~40 RMB; leash typically included
pricing_sessions.coaching_fee: Group lesson 200-400 RMB per person; private 1-on-1 lesson 500-800 RMB for ~2 hours; multi-day camps available at GROM Surf Camp and Shaka Surf Club
pricing_sessions.coaching_packages: Multi-day surf camps (3-5 days) ~1,500-3,500 RMB at major schools (e.g., GROM Surf Camp, Shaka); video analysis and photo packages 200-500 RMB add-on [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.membership_program: Most schools sell punch cards (10-session) at ~2,000-3,500 RMB; no formal bay-wide membership [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.corporate_team_building: Several surf clubs (Shaka, GROM) accept team-building bookings; minimum group sizes typically 10-20 people; pricing on request [uncertain]

experience_guide.best_season: October to March (NE monsoon swell season); peak November-February with ~80% surfable days
experience_guide.best_swell_window: October-March: NE swells 0.8-2.5 m, period 7-12 s, offshore NW winds; secondary typhoon swells possible August-September
experience_guide.crowd_level: Moderate to crowded in peak winter season (Nov-Feb), especially at Main Point during competitions; uncrowded in shoulder months and at Beachie; weekday mornings quietest
experience_guide.booking_method: Walk-in at any beachside surf club; online booking via Dianping (大眾點評), WeChat mini-programs of individual clubs (Shaka, GROM, Hi Surf, etc.), or Trip.com/Ctrip for surf camps
experience_guide.booking_platform: Dianping (大眾點評), Meituan (美團), Xiaohongshu (小紅書) for shop discovery, WeChat mini-programs, Trip.com / Ctrip (攜程)
experience_guide.payment_options: WeChat Pay and Alipay universally accepted; cash accepted at most shops; international credit cards rarely accepted at small clubs (only at PerfectSwell resort and large hotels)
experience_guide.skill_required: All levels welcome; Beachie suits zero-base beginners; Main Point requires intermediate paddling and pop-up; Ghost Hotel for advanced only
experience_guide.age_eligibility: Most surf schools accept ages 6+ for kids' lessons; no formal upper age limit; participants must be able to swim
experience_guide.child_friendly_score: 3/5 - several schools offer kids programs and soft-top boards; Beachie sandbar is shallow and beginner-friendly, but no enclosed kids' pool
experience_guide.sup_paddleboard_offering: Yes - SUP rentals and lessons available at multiple Hexin/Tianxin village clubs
experience_guide.photography_video_service: Photo/video service offered by most clubs; typical pricing 100-300 RMB for session photos, 300-580 RMB for edited video [uncertain]
experience_guide.language_support: Limited English at most local clubs; Shaka Surf Club, GROM Surf Camp, and a few foreign-owned shops have English-speaking instructors; Mandarin dominant
experience_guide.accessibility_for_disabled: No formal adaptive surfing program; beach access is sandy with no boardwalks [uncertain]
experience_guide.stance_preference_input: Not required when booking; stance does not affect access (point break is left-hander, surfable by both stances)
experience_guide.safety_notes: Strong rips in winter; lifeguards present at main beach but not all sections; ISA-certified coaches available at top clubs; surf insurance recommended; competition periods may close certain breaks
experience_guide.events_competitions_calendar: Annual host of National Surfing Championships (中國全國衝浪錦標賽); WSL Wanning Corona Cero Pro QS 4000 (Dec 11-17, 2025 - first WSL return since 2020); historical host of WSL Longboard World Championship 2011-2020, Swatch Girls Pro, ISA China Cup, Hainan Open

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: Tianxin Village (田新村) and Hexin Village offer 50+ surf hostels and guesthouses (民宿) ranging from 150-500 RMB/night; mid-range resorts on the bay 600-1,500 RMB; Le Meridien Shimei Bay and other 5-star resorts 1,000-3,000 RMB nearby
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: PerfectSwell Riyue Bay Surf Resort (opened Nov 11, 2025) offers themed surf resort rooms; GROM Surf Camp and Shaka Surf Club operate on-site lodges; Forest Inn beachfront accommodation [uncertain pricing]
surroundings.nearby_food: Surf village cafes (GROM Music Bar, Surf House Cafe), Hainanese seafood restaurants, fresh coconut and Wenchang chicken stands, BBQ stalls; per-person spend 50-150 RMB
surroundings.transport: Self-drive via Hainan East Ring Highway (G98); high-speed train to Shenzhou Station (~10 km) or Wanning Station (~30 km) plus taxi/Didi; free local electric shuttle 8:00-18:00 within bay area
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: ~1.5 hours / ~110 km drive from Sanya Phoenix International Airport (SYX) via G98 East Ring Highway; ~3 hours / ~190 km from Haikou Meilan International Airport (HAK); high-speed rail to Shenzhou Station ~15 minutes from bay
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Riyue Bay surf + Shenzhou Peninsula (神州半島) resort + Xinglong Tropical Botanical Garden (興隆熱帶植物園) + Shimei Bay (石梅灣); 3-5 day Wanning surf trip itinerary common

pros: China's premier surf destination with most consistent winter swell (~200+ surfable days/year)
Diverse breaks suit all levels: Beachie for beginners, Main Point for intermediate, Ghost Hotel for advanced
Dense surf cluster of 50+ clubs in Tianxin Village creates affordable, vibrant surf community
National training base credibility; ISA/WSL-sanctioned competitions hosted annually
Warm water year-round (avg 26.5C), no wetsuit needed most of the year
cons: Crowds can be heavy at Main Point in peak winter and during competitions
Strong rips in winter pose risk for inexperienced surfers
Limited English support outside flagship schools
Travel time from Sanya/Haikou (1.5-3 hours) discourages day trips
Summer surf inconsistent except during typhoon swells; some flat days
reviews_reputation.user_reviews: Trip.com / Tripadvisor: ~4.0-4.5/5 average; Xiaohongshu (小紅書) heavily features Riyue Bay as top mainland surf spot; Dianping reviews praise wave consistency and surf-village vibe but note crowding and basic guesthouse standards
reviews_reputation.recommended_for: Intermediate to advanced surfers seeking genuine ocean waves; surf-trip travelers; competition viewers; longboarders (long lefts); surf-camp groups
reviews_reputation.operator_brand_partnership: China National Surfing Team training base (under General Administration of Sport of China); WSL international event partnership (returned 2025); ISA China Cup partner; new PerfectSwell resort developed by China Tourism Group (CTG) using American Wave Machines (AWM) PerfectSwell technology

basic_info.name: Shimei Bay & Shenzhou Peninsula / 萬寧·石梅灣 與 神州半島
basic_info.location: Shimei Bay (石梅湾) and Shenzhou Peninsula (神州半岛, Dong'ao town area), Wanning City, Hainan Province; ~10-20 km north of Riyue Bay along Hainan east coast; ~100-110 km north of Sanya, ~3 hours south of Haikou; both bays accessed via Hainan East Ring Highway (G98) and high-speed Shenzhou Railway Station
basic_info.operator: Multi-resort destinations - no single surf operator; Shimei Bay anchored by Le Meridien Shimei Bay (开维 Le Meridien) and The Westin Shimei Bay Resort plus Aloft and Sheraton; Shenzhou Peninsula anchored by Grand Hyatt Shenzhou Peninsula Resort, Le Meridien Shenzhou Peninsula and Howard Johnson; surf services run by independent pro surf shops on each beach; broader Wanning surf cluster managed under Wanning Tourism Bureau as alternative breaks to flagship Riyue Bay
basic_info.opening_date: N/A (natural surf spots; both areas developed as resort/leisure destinations from late 2000s; surf scene secondary to Riyue Bay since ~2015)

technical_specs.wave_technology_brand: N/A (natural waves)
technical_specs.pool_size: Shimei Bay: ~6 km arcing silver-sand beach with primary surf zone at adjacent Yangkou Bay (洋口湾) ~15 minutes west of main bay; Shenzhou Peninsula: ~10 km of east-facing sandy beaches surrounded by sea on three sides with multiple peak setups [uncertain exact lengths]
technical_specs.wave_height: Shimei Bay: seasonal average 0.4-1.2 m, gentle wave-kindergarten waters; Shenzhou Peninsula: 0.5-1.5 m typical, can build to 2 m in winter NE monsoon, summer 0.3-0.8 m; both lower-energy than adjacent Riyue Bay's point breaks
technical_specs.wave_height_adjustability: Not adjustable (natural ocean swell driven by NE monsoon Oct-Mar and occasional summer typhoon swells)
technical_specs.wave_types: Shimei Bay: predominantly mellow beach break over flat seafloor producing small, regular, beginner-friendly waves (locally nicknamed 'wave kindergarten' 浪花幼儿园); Shenzhou Peninsula: mix of beach break and minor point setups - sandy bottoms with consistent peelers; both bays NE-facing pick up the same NE monsoon swell as Riyue Bay but with smaller, less powerful waves
technical_specs.waves_per_hour: N/A (natural surf spot)
technical_specs.wave_count_per_set: N/A (natural ocean sets, typically 3-5 waves per set with 8-12 second intervals on NE monsoon days) [uncertain]
technical_specs.session_length: N/A (open ocean access; surf-school lessons typically 90-120 minutes when offered by mobile coaches from Riyue Bay)
technical_specs.stance_rotation_schedule: N/A (beach breaks offer both lefts and rights; no daily stance rotation needed)
technical_specs.wave_type_classification: Predominantly beach break over sand at both Shimei Bay and Shenzhou Peninsula; some minor point/cove setups on the peninsula's southern tip; secondary alternative breaks for advanced surfers when Riyue Bay is over-crowded or wind-blown out

pricing_sessions.price_range: Free open-ocean access at both bays; lessons less standardized than at Riyue/Houhai - typical 300-500 RMB for 2-hour group lesson; private lessons 500-800 RMB; resort-arranged surf experiences via Le Meridien/Westin/Grand Hyatt 600-1,200 RMB including transfer [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.session_skill_tiers: Limited tiered structure; mostly beginner group lessons (~300-400 RMB) at Shimei Bay's wave-kindergarten zone; intermediate/advanced surfers typically self-guide and seek the alternative spots when Riyue Bay is too crowded; resort surf packages combine lesson + photography [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.equipment_rental: Soft-top board ~100-200 RMB/day; resort hotels (Le Meridien Shimei Bay, Grand Hyatt Shenzhou) provide complimentary or paid surf board rental for guests; SUP and kayak rentals widely offered; rashguard typically included with lesson [uncertain pricing]
pricing_sessions.coaching_fee: Group lesson 300-500 RMB per person (2 hours); private 1-on-1 lesson 500-800 RMB; mobile coaches often travel from Riyue Bay's Tianxin Village cluster; resort-arranged sessions priced higher with concierge surcharge [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.coaching_packages: Multi-day surf retreats less common than at Riyue Bay; resort + surf packages bundled by Le Meridien Shimei Bay and Grand Hyatt Shenzhou; photo/video add-on 200-500 RMB; multi-day intermediate camps 1,500-3,500 RMB through Riyue-Bay-based schools (GROM, Shaka) using these as overflow breaks [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.membership_program: No formal bay-wide membership; resort hotel loyalty programs (Marriott Bonvoy at Le Meridien/Westin/Sheraton, World of Hyatt at Grand Hyatt Shenzhou) provide booking benefits; some Riyue-Bay-based clubs sell 10-session punch cards usable at multiple bays [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.corporate_team_building: Resort properties (Westin, Le Meridien, Grand Hyatt) actively market beach team-building, MICE packages and corporate retreats; Shenzhou Peninsula hosts annual large-scale events (China Beach Ultimate Open with 200+ players Sep 20-21, 2025) demonstrating capacity; minimums 20+ people typical [uncertain]

experience_guide.best_season: October to April for surf (NE monsoon, ~80% surf days Nov-Feb); July-August occasional bigger swells for advanced surfers; year-round resort/beach holiday destination with average water 26C
experience_guide.best_swell_window: October-March: NE monsoon swells 0.5-2.0 m, period 7-12 s, offshore-cross winds; summer south-swell windows April-October produce smaller (~30% surf days) but cleaner days; typhoon swells Aug-Sep can deliver larger waves but often blown out
experience_guide.crowd_level: Significantly less crowded than Riyue Bay or Houhai year-round - both bays attract beach-resort tourists rather than dedicated surfers; Shenzhou Peninsula slightly busier due to Grand Hyatt and event venues; Shimei Bay especially uncrowded outside Le Meridien stretch; advanced surfers use these as overflow when Riyue Bay too crowded
experience_guide.booking_method: Surf lessons booked via resort concierge at Le Meridien Shimei Bay, Westin Shimei Bay, Grand Hyatt Shenzhou Peninsula, or directly through Riyue-Bay-based mobile coaches via WeChat; Trip.com/Ctrip and Klook list activity packages; walk-in surf rentals available on beach during peak season
experience_guide.booking_platform: Trip.com / Ctrip (携程), Marriott Bonvoy / Hyatt apps for resort packages, Dianping (大众点评), Meituan (美团), Xiaohongshu (小红书), WeChat mini-programs of mobile coaches, Klook
experience_guide.payment_options: WeChat Pay and Alipay universally accepted; cash accepted; international credit cards accepted at Le Meridien, Westin, Sheraton, Aloft, Grand Hyatt and other 5-star resorts (key advantage over Riyue/Houhai for foreign visitors); small beach vendors WeChat/Alipay only
experience_guide.skill_required: Shimei Bay wave-kindergarten waters suit zero-base beginners; Shenzhou Peninsula suits beginner to intermediate; both serve advanced surfers seeking quieter alternative when Riyue Bay over-crowded; not the spot for true big-wave or expert tube hunting
experience_guide.age_eligibility: Resort-organized lessons typically accept ages 6+ with parental supervision; no formal upper age limit; participants must be able to swim; resort kids' clubs supervise younger children on beach activities
experience_guide.child_friendly_score: 4/5 - both bays have shallow sandy entry, beach-resort amenities, kids' clubs at Grand Hyatt Shenzhou and Le Meridien Shimei; cleaner and quieter than Houhai; some debris on outer non-resort beach sections
experience_guide.sup_paddleboard_offering: Yes - SUP and kayak rentals available at all major resorts and at the beach; Shenzhou Peninsula popular for SUP given consistent gentle conditions; resort water-sport huts also offer parasailing, jet ski, banana boat
experience_guide.photography_video_service: Resort photographers and independent freelancers offer beach/surf shoots ~300-800 RMB; surf-lesson packages may include GoPro footage; drone shoots arranged via concierge ~500-1,000 RMB [uncertain]
experience_guide.language_support: English support strong at all 5-star resorts (Le Meridien, Westin, Sheraton, Aloft, Grand Hyatt, Le Meridien Shenzhou); limited English at independent beach surf vendors; Mandarin dominant outside resort property
experience_guide.accessibility_for_disabled: Resort properties (Westin, Grand Hyatt) provide accessible rooms and lobby ramps; beach access not formally certified; no adaptive surfing program documented [uncertain]
experience_guide.stance_preference_input: Not required when booking; beach breaks accommodate both stances
experience_guide.safety_notes: Generally safer than Riyue Bay's stronger point breaks; sandy bottoms, no significant reef hazards; lifeguards on resort-managed beaches during operating hours; rip currents can develop in winter NE monsoon; sun exposure intense year-round; resorts offer insured water-sport packages
experience_guide.events_competitions_calendar: Shenzhou Peninsula hosts China Beach Ultimate Open (Sep 20-21, 2025) and various beach Xgames events; Shimei Bay used for music festivals, wedding events, and Sheraton/Westin MICE programs; not on WSL/ISA surf circuit (those go to Riyue Bay); occasional national surf-team training and qualifier events at peninsula's southern peaks [uncertain]

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: Shimei Bay: The Westin Shimei Bay Resort (~700-1,500 RMB), Le Meridien Shimei Bay Beach Resort & Spa (~1,000-2,000 RMB; appeared in 'If You Are the One 2'), Sheraton Shimei Bay Resort, Aloft Shimei Bay; budget guesthouses in Huarun residential area 10-15 min walk to beach (~200-400 RMB); Shenzhou Peninsula: Grand Hyatt Shenzhou Peninsula Resort (~1,500-3,000 RMB), Le Meridien Shenzhou Peninsula Resort, Howard Johnson Resort Shenzhou Peninsula, Sheraton Shenzhou Peninsula
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: All surf-adjacent stays are integrated 5-star resort hotels (no surf-house lodge culture as at Riyue/Houhai); surf gear stored or rented through resort water-sports concierge; nearest surf-camp lodging is at Tianxin Village in Riyue Bay ~15-30 km away [uncertain pricing details]
surroundings.nearby_food: Resort restaurants (Westin Latitude, Le Meridien Latest Recipe, Grand Hyatt international and Cantonese restaurants); Shimei Bay 400 m commercial area with seafood, Sichuan, noodle and bakery options plus Phoenix Nine-Li Bookhouse cafe; Wanning specialties: Hele crab, Dongshan mutton, Houan chicken, fresh coconut; per-person spend 100-500 RMB at resorts, 50-150 RMB at village restaurants
surroundings.transport: Self-drive via Hainan East Ring Highway (G98); high-speed train to Shenzhou Station (35 min from main bay) or Wanning Station (more onward connections); resort shuttle services from rail stations (book in advance); Didi/taxi from local towns; electric bike rentals on Shenzhou Peninsula scenic road
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: Shimei Bay: ~100 km / 1.5 hour drive from Sanya Phoenix International Airport (SYX); ~190 km / 2.5 hour drive from Haikou Meilan International Airport (HAK); Shenzhou Peninsula: ~110 km / 2 hour drive from SYX, ~180 km / 2.5 hour from HAK; high-speed rail to Shenzhou Station ~35 min from Sanya, ~1 hour from Haikou
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Wanning surf-resort triangle: Riyue Bay (surf training base) + Shimei Bay (wave kindergarten + Le Meridien luxury) + Shenzhou Peninsula (Grand Hyatt + lighthouse + scenic coastal road); combine with Xinglong Tropical Botanical Garden (兴隆热带植物园) and Dongshan Ridge (东山岭); typical 3-5 day Wanning resort + surf itinerary

pros: Significantly less crowded than Riyue Bay or Houhai - peaceful waves and beaches
Luxury 5-star resort infrastructure (Le Meridien, Westin, Grand Hyatt) with English support, international cards and amenities
Shimei Bay 'wave kindergarten' truly safe for absolute beginners and small children
Pristine 6 km silver sand beach (Shimei) and palm-lined scenic coastal road (Shenzhou Peninsula)
Easy alternative breaks for intermediate/advanced surfers when Riyue Bay is over-crowded or wind-blown
cons: Far fewer surf clubs and instructors than Riyue Bay or Houhai - lessons must be pre-arranged
Waves smaller and less powerful than Riyue Bay's flagship point breaks - not ideal for advanced wave hunters
No surf-village vibe - mostly resort guests and beach tourists rather than surf community
Some non-resort beach sections accumulate debris and lack lifeguard coverage
Higher overall cost when staying at flagship resorts (1,000-3,000 RMB/night)
reviews_reputation.user_reviews: Trip.com / Tripadvisor: Shimei Bay 4.0-4.5/5 (rated 'Hainan's Most Beautiful Bay' by China National Geographic), Shenzhou Peninsula 4.0-4.5/5; Xiaohongshu features as quieter resort alternative to Sanya; Dianping reviews praise beach quality and hotel service, note distance from Sanya as drawback; surf-specific reviews label both as 'overflow' or 'kindergarten' breaks for beginners and intermediates seeking refuge from Riyue Bay crowds
reviews_reputation.recommended_for: Beginner-to-intermediate surfers seeking quieter alternative to Riyue Bay; family resort holidays; couples on luxury beach escape; advanced surfers using as backup when Riyue Bay crowded or blown out; corporate retreat / MICE groups; international visitors needing English-speaking resort infrastructure
reviews_reputation.operator_brand_partnership: Shimei Bay anchored by Marriott group (Le Meridien, Westin, Sheraton, Aloft); Shenzhou Peninsula anchored by Hyatt (Grand Hyatt) and Marriott (Le Meridien, Sheraton); both bays jointly promoted by Wanning Tourism Bureau as 'Wanning Surf Triangle' alongside Riyue Bay; surf services typically subcontracted to mobile coaches from Riyue Bay Tianxin Village cluster (GROM, Shaka, Hi Surf) [uncertain partnership details]

basic_info.name: SURFLAND Wanning Surf Resort (SURFLAND 萬寧逐浪度假區 / 中旅逐浪度假區)
basic_info.location: No. 1 Yingbin First Road, Riyue Bay Tourism Area, Liji Town, Wanning City, Hainan Province, China (海南省萬寧市禮紀鎮日月灣遊覽區迎賓一路1號)
basic_info.operator: China Tourism Group (中旅集團 / China Travel Group, state-owned) via its commercial-management arm Zhonglu Shangguan; wave technology supplied and co-operated with American Wave Machines (AWM)

technical_specs.wave_technology_brand: PerfectSwell by American Wave Machines (AWM) - 30-chamber pneumatic system, the only one of its kind globally; first PerfectSwell installation in mainland China
technical_specs.pool_size: Approximately 10,000 m² wave basin within a ~155-acre (≈63 ha) overall resort footprint
technical_specs.wave_height: 0.3-2.7 m (waves above 2 m meet Olympic competition standards)
technical_specs.wave_height_adjustability: Yes - 23 programmable wave configurations/patterns covering everything from soft whitewater for beginners to large barrels for pros; both left- and right-hand waves can be generated on demand
technical_specs.wave_types: Artificial PerfectSwell waves spanning beginner whitewater, intermediate green/peeling shoulders, advanced steep faces, and pro-level barrels/aerial sections
technical_specs.waves_per_hour: Up to ~600 waves per hour (one wave every 6 seconds when running continuously) [uncertain]
technical_specs.wave_count_per_set: 4 waves per set, ~6 seconds between waves within a set
technical_specs.session_length: Approximately 55-60 minutes per public session (matches Surfway Hainan in-house school session length); private bookings sold in 1-hour and 2-hour blocks
technical_specs.stance_rotation_schedule: Yes - the pool alternates daily between Goofy and Regular orientation (one day Goofy / next day Regular); guests must check the date on the booking calendar before reserving
technical_specs.wave_type_classification: Artificial PerfectSwell (pneumatic) - waves behave like a clean point/reef peel with predictable take-off zones, making board choice closer to a point break than a beach break

pricing_sessions.price_range: Group sessions start at 388-545 RMB per 55-minute slot (board and rashguard rental extra); private 1-hour bookings range from ~7,760 RMB (Intermediate) to ~10,150 RMB (PRO/barrels), 2-hour bookings up to ~12,160 RMB
pricing_sessions.session_skill_tiers: Beginner 398 RMB, Intermediate 388 RMB, Advanced 498 RMB, PRO 545 RMB (per 55-minute group session, as published by the in-house Surfway Hainan school)
pricing_sessions.equipment_rental: Hardtop surfboard (basic) 80 RMB/session with 500 RMB deposit; Hardtop surfboard (pro) 300 RMB/day; wetsuit 40 RMB with 100 RMB deposit
pricing_sessions.coaching_fee: Land coaching + in-water coaching combo 600 RMB; wave-pushing assistance 328 RMB; full-day private ocean lesson 900 RMB [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.coaching_packages: Complete package (land coaching + in-water coaching + filming + video analysis) 1,175 RMB; video analysis + filming bundle 580 RMB; standalone professional filming 520 RMB; drone filming with 3 batteries 750 RMB
pricing_sessions.membership_program: No publicly published annual/monthly/10-pack membership card as of opening; multi-session and corporate packages handled on request [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.corporate_team_building: Yes - resort markets group bookings, private pool rentals and team-building packages; specific minimum head-count and pricing not publicly listed [uncertain]

experience_guide.best_season: Year-round operation (the wave pool is weather-independent); peak demand aligns with Hainan's high tourist season November-April, plus summer school holidays July-August
experience_guide.best_swell_window: N/A - wave pool is mechanically generated; the surrounding natural Riyue Bay break is best October to March
experience_guide.crowd_level: Heavy demand and long wait times reported during the November 2025 soft-opening and major holidays; 4-wave sets create natural queues; weekday off-peak sessions are noticeably calmer
experience_guide.booking_method: Session-based online reservation; date-specific (must match Goofy/Regular rotation calendar); walk-ins limited
experience_guide.booking_platform: Ctrip / Trip.com (携程), Dianping (大眾點評), official WeChat mini-program; international users can also book via Surfway Hainan (in-house school) by WhatsApp, Telegram or email
experience_guide.payment_options: Alipay, WeChat Pay, Chinese bank cards; international Visa/Mastercard accepted through the Surfway Hainan in-house school for foreign visitors; cash generally not preferred
experience_guide.skill_required: All levels - Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced and PRO tiers; sessions are strictly tier-segregated for safety and wave settings
experience_guide.age_eligibility: Recommended ages 6-60; minimum height ~1.0 m; under-6 and over-60 generally not admitted to in-water sessions for safety reasons
experience_guide.child_friendly_score: 4 out of 5 - dedicated beginner sessions, soft-top boards, on-site themed children's hotel rooms and family-oriented amenities; the 6-year minimum and height limit exclude very young kids
experience_guide.sup_paddleboard_offering: Limited inside the wave pool itself; SUP and paddleboard rentals widely available at the adjacent natural Riyue Bay beach [uncertain]
experience_guide.photography_video_service: 3 complimentary still photos included with each session; dynamic video upgrade 199 RMB; professional filming add-on 520 RMB; drone filming (3 batteries) 750 RMB; full filming + analysis bundle 580-1,175 RMB
experience_guide.language_support: Mandarin Chinese is primary; English-speaking personal coordinator available through the Surfway Hainan school; AWM-affiliated international staff also on site
experience_guide.accessibility_for_disabled: No publicly documented adaptive surfing program or barrier-free in-water access at opening; resort common areas (hotels, retail street) follow standard Chinese accessibility codes [uncertain]
experience_guide.stance_preference_input: Yes - because the pool runs Goofy one day and Regular the next, guests must indicate stance and pick a date that matches; calendar shown at booking time
experience_guide.safety_notes: On-site lifeguards and safety briefing before each session; ISA-trained / ISA Level-2 coaches present (Riyue Bay area is the training base of China's National Surfing Team and home to a generation of ISA-certified coaches); insurance recommended; helmets typically required for advanced/PRO settings
experience_guide.events_competitions_calendar: Designed as China's first Olympic-grade competition venue and intended host for national-team training and future ISA / WSL-sanctioned events; opening week (Nov 2025) featured international surf demos, drone shows, skate exchange races and a 100-person fun run; specific 2026 competition calendar not yet published [uncertain]

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: Numerous Riyue Bay surf villas, guesthouses and boutique hotels (e.g., Sweetome Surf Hotel, Forest Inn Riyue Bay Surf Branch, Riyue Bay Legend Hotel) priced ~300-1,200 RMB/night; design-forward villas and Airbnbs available in the wider Liji Town surf village
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: Two on-site hotels with 132 rooms total: (1) Yue Chao Surf-Themed Hotel (越潮 / 樾潮 - China's first surf-themed hotel, family-friendly, pet-friendly, with constant-temperature pool and complimentary 1-hour dual surf lesson); (2) Yu Yue Designer Hotel (御越 / 屿玥). Garden-view rooms from ~780 RMB and sea-view rooms from ~980 RMB (breakfast included)
surroundings.nearby_food: On-site X-LIVIN Street (逐浪越界潮流商業街區) features restaurants, a beachfront club, live-music venue and cafés; surrounding Riyue Bay surf village offers Hainanese seafood, surf-shack cafés, hotpot and local Wanning specialties
surroundings.transport: Self-drive recommended; ride-hailing (Didi) and resort shuttles available; taxis from Shenzhou High-Speed Rail Station ~20 minutes; private car charters from Sanya/Haikou widely available
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: Approximately 70 minutes by car from Sanya Phoenix International Airport (SYX); ~18.4 km / ~20-minute drive from Shenzhou High-Speed Railway Station on the Hainan Eastern Ring HSR; ~2 hours from Haikou Meilan International Airport (HAK)
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Riyue Bay surf village + SURFLAND wave pool + natural Riyue Bay beach surf + Xinglong Tropical Botanical Garden + Shenzhou Peninsula resorts + Sun-Moon Bay Buddhist sites; multi-day surf-focused itineraries pair AM ocean session with PM wave-pool session

reviews_reputation.pros: 1) China's first Olympic-grade artificial wave pool with genuine 0.3-2.7 m programmable waves; 2) PerfectSwell's 30-chamber pneumatic tech delivers very consistent, ocean-like waves; 3) Year-round, weather-proof operation with both Goofy and Regular days; 4) On-site hotels (132 rooms), retail street, dining and skate park make it a full destination; 5) Professional ISA-trained coaching staff and integration with China's national team training base
reviews_reputation.cons: 1) Long wait times during opening period and peak holidays - 4-wave sets create queues; 2) Pricing is steep relative to natural ocean lessons in the same bay (388-545 RMB group, up to 12,160 RMB private); 3) Strict 6-60 age band and height limit excludes very young children and senior surfers; 4) Goofy/Regular daily rotation forces stance-aware date planning; 5) Far from major airports (~70 min from Sanya) requires advance transport planning
reviews_reputation.user_reviews: Affiliated Yue Chao hotel scoring 4.8/5 with ~392 reviews on Ctrip; early Xiaohongshu and travel-blog reviews praise wave quality ("wave is real, not foam") and resort design but flag long queues, high prices and the 4-wave-per-set pacing; ranked No. 6 in Wanning luxury hotel category shortly after opening
reviews_reputation.recommended_for: Intermediate-to-advanced surfers seeking consistent quality waves; families with children aged 6+; couples and group travelers wanting a one-stop surf-resort experience; corporate team-building groups; photographers and content creators; pro surfers training for competition
reviews_reputation.operator_brand_partnership: China Tourism Group (中旅集團, 中國旅遊集團) - state-owned investor and operator (via Zhonglu Shangguan / 中旅商管) - paired with American Wave Machines (AWM, San Diego) which licenses and co-operates the PerfectSwell technology; total reported investment ~1 billion RMB

verification.verification_sources: 1) China Daily Hainan (hain.chinadaily.com.cn) opening announcement, 2025-11-11; 2) Hotel News Resource and Korea Herald wire releases, Oct 2025; 3) WavePool Magazine (wavepoolmag.com) wave-testing report, Oct 2025; 4) Tropical Hainan article on Olympic-standard wave pool opening; 5) Surfway Hainan official site (surfway-hainan.com) for in-house school pricing; 6) Vast Surf Shop in-depth review blog (vastsurfshop.com/blogs/blog/surfland); 7) Ctrip listing for 万宁日月湾中旅逐浪樾潮酒店 (hotel ID 132247344); 8) Travel Tomorrow and Travel And Tour World coverage; 9) Hainan local newspaper (zqdb.com.cn) Nov-13 PDF; 10) The Inertia surf news coverage

basic_info.name: Surfplus Surf School / 冲浪家社区·SURFPLUS 冲浪学校 (Riyue Bay Branch)
basic_info.location: No. 1 Nanhai Fishing Village, Riyue Bay, Liji Town, Wanning City, Hainan Province, China (海南省萬寧市禮紀鎮日月灣南海漁村1號)
basic_info.operator: Surfplus / 冲浪家 - independent surf-community brand co-founded by a group of surf enthusiasts; operates the on-site Surfplus surf school + Surfplus Beach Bar coffee/bar concept; community-driven 'surf + beach lifestyle' model
basic_info.opening_date: N/A (natural-ocean surf school established in the Riyue Bay surf community circa 2018-2020) [uncertain]

technical_specs.wave_technology_brand: N/A (natural ocean surf school using Riyue Bay's beach break and point break)
technical_specs.pool_size: N/A - operates in the open ocean at Riyue Bay (~5-7 km of surfable coastline shared with 50+ other clubs)
technical_specs.wave_height: Seasonal average 0.6-2.0 m at Riyue Bay; winter peaks (Oct-Mar) regularly produce 1.5-2 m sets, summer/monsoon waves typically 0.5-1.2 m
technical_specs.wave_height_adjustability: Not adjustable (natural ocean swell)
technical_specs.wave_types: Mix of beach break (sandbar at Beachie / Nanhai Fishing Village frontage for beginner classes) and adjacent left-hand point break (Main Point / Ghost Hotel for intermediate-advanced students)
technical_specs.waves_per_hour: N/A (natural ocean)
technical_specs.wave_count_per_set: N/A (natural ocean sets, typically 3-6 waves per set with 8-15 second intervals)
technical_specs.session_length: 120 minutes per class session (covers ~30 min land theory + ~90 min water training); group sessions can extend with bundled multi-session packages
technical_specs.stance_rotation_schedule: N/A (natural ocean - no daily rotation; left-hand point break works frontside for Goofy, backside for Regular)
technical_specs.wave_type_classification: Beach break (primary teaching zone) plus point break (for progression); suits soft-top funboards for beginners, longboards / shortboards for intermediates

pricing_sessions.price_range: Group beginner class 420 RMB per person (3-person class, 2 hours); 1-on-2 (couple/friends) and 1-on-1 private classes available - pricing typically 600-1,000+ RMB per person depending on tier
pricing_sessions.session_skill_tiers: Beginner 3-person group: 420 RMB / 2 hr (includes 30 min theory + 90 min water practice); 1-on-2 progression class: 2 sessions x 120 min in one day, includes equipment, photography, ISA 1-star assessment and certification fee [uncertain on exact RMB]; 1-on-1 private class pricing on request
pricing_sessions.equipment_rental: Surfboard, rashguard / wetsuit (冲浪服), leash, sunscreen mud (防曬泥) and freshwater shower / locker included in lesson fees; standalone board rental ~80-150 RMB/session, soft-top day rental ~150-250 RMB [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.coaching_fee: Group coaching included in 420 RMB beginner package; 1-on-2 progression coaching ~580-780 RMB per person [uncertain]; 1-on-1 private coaching ~800-1,200 RMB per session [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.coaching_packages: Multi-session progression bundles linked to ISA 1-star and 2-star certification; complete package includes equipment, photography, video and certification; multi-day surf camps available on request [uncertain on packages structure]
pricing_sessions.membership_program: No publicly listed annual or 10-pack membership card; multi-session discounts handled on request via WeChat customer service [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.corporate_team_building: Yes - club accepts team-building events and group bookings; can host beach BBQ + surf intro packages combined with the on-site Surfplus Beach Bar; minimum group size and pricing case by case [uncertain]

experience_guide.best_season: October to March (NE monsoon swell season at Riyue Bay) for intermediate and advanced students; Apr-Sep gentler conditions ideal for total beginners and kids
experience_guide.best_swell_window: October-March: NE swells 0.8-2.5 m, period 7-12 s, offshore NW winds; secondary typhoon swells possible August-September
experience_guide.crowd_level: Beginner classes can fill quickly during Spring Festival, May Day, National Day and weekend holidays; weekday mornings and shoulder months (May, Sep) are quietest; lineup at Riyue Bay generally crowded at Main Point in winter
experience_guide.booking_method: Direct booking via WeChat (官方微信), Dianping (大眾點評), Meituan (美團), Ctrip (攜程), Qunar (去哪兒); walk-in possible at the Nanhai Fishing Village shopfront subject to coach availability
experience_guide.booking_platform: Dianping (大眾點評), Meituan (美團), Ctrip / Trip.com (攜程), Qunar (去哪兒), Xiaohongshu (小紅書), WeChat mini-program / official account, Instagram @surfplus.china
experience_guide.payment_options: WeChat Pay and Alipay (default); cash accepted; international credit cards generally not accepted at the small-shop level
experience_guide.skill_required: All levels - dedicated zero-base Beginner curriculum (theory + balance + paddling + pop-up); intermediate progression to green-wave shoulders; advanced 1-on-1 coaching for point-break technique
experience_guide.age_eligibility: Typically 8-60 years for adult classes; kids' classes available for ages 6-12 with soft-top boards; participants must be able to swim 50 m comfortably [uncertain on exact lower age limit]
experience_guide.child_friendly_score: 3 out of 5 - kids' soft-tops and patient coaches available, beachfront cafe lets parents wait comfortably, but no dedicated kids' enclosed pool and no minimum-height waiver; better suited to age 8+
experience_guide.sup_paddleboard_offering: Not listed as a primary Surfplus offering; SUP rentals available from neighboring Riyue Bay clubs and shops [uncertain]
experience_guide.photography_video_service: Photography and video included in the 1-on-2 progression package; standalone photo/video add-on typically 100-300 RMB; complimentary on-site photos for some private bookings
experience_guide.language_support: Mandarin Chinese primary; some English support from younger coaches and the cafe staff (school's social presence includes an English-language Instagram @surfplus.china); not as foreigner-oriented as Surfway Hainan
experience_guide.accessibility_for_disabled: No publicly listed adaptive surfing program; beach access is sandy with no boardwalks [uncertain]
experience_guide.stance_preference_input: Not required when booking (natural ocean - stance assessed during land theory and adjusted during water session)
experience_guide.safety_notes: Coaches drawn from the Riyue Bay surf community; mandatory pre-session safety briefing; lifeguards on the main beach during posted hours; surf insurance recommended; reschedule must be made >24 h in advance, <24 h is non-refundable
experience_guide.events_competitions_calendar: Surfplus is a community-style school not a competition organizer, but hosts internal Surfplus surf jams, beach parties at Surfplus Beach Bar, and ISA 1-star / 2-star assessment days; students often participate in National Surfing Championships and the Wanning WSL Corona Cero Pro QS held at Riyue Bay each Dec [uncertain on internal jam schedule]

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: Riyue Bay surf-village guesthouses (民宿) 200-500 RMB/night; Sweetome Surf Hotel, Forest Inn Riyue Bay Surf Branch ~400-900 RMB; mid-range resort 600-1,500 RMB; SURFLAND on-resort hotels Yue Chao 越潮 + Yu Yue 御越 from 780 RMB; Le Meridien Shimei Bay 1,000-3,000 RMB
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: Surfplus does not directly operate hotel rooms; the Nanhai Fishing Village location is co-located with various small surf guesthouses and apartments. Surfplus Beach Bar (cafe / bar / restaurant on the same premises) provides on-site dining and lounging for guests [uncertain on integrated lodging]
surroundings.nearby_food: Surfplus Beach Bar on-site (cafe / bar serving coffee, light meals, beachside drinks - rated on Ctrip food channel); Riyue Bay surf village cafes (GROM Music Bar, Surf House Cafe), Hainanese seafood restaurants, BBQ stalls, Wenchang chicken; per-person spend 50-150 RMB
surroundings.transport: Self-drive via Hainan East Ring Expressway G98; high-speed train to Shenzhou Station (~10 km / 15 min taxi) or Wanning Station (~30 km); Didi / taxi widely available; free local Riyue Bay electric shuttle 8:00-18:00
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: ~1.5 hours / ~110 km drive from Sanya Phoenix International Airport (SYX); ~3 hours / ~190 km from Haikou Meilan International Airport (HAK); high-speed rail to Shenzhou Station ~15 minutes from the bay
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Surfplus AM lesson + Riyue Bay free surf + Surfplus Beach Bar coffee/sunset + Shenzhou Peninsula resort visit + Xinglong Tropical Botanical Garden + Shimei Bay; complementary to SURFLAND PerfectSwell pool at Riyue Bay (combine pool + ocean for a learn-to-surf weekend)

pros: Long-established Wanning surf-community brand with strong local cred at Riyue Bay's Nanhai Fishing Village
Combined surf school + Surfplus Beach Bar creates a relaxed 'surf + lifestyle' hangout with food/drink between sessions
Affordable group-class pricing (420 RMB / 2 hr) compared to artificial-pool alternatives like SURFLAND
Includes equipment, wetsuit, photography, sunscreen, freshwater shower and ISA 1-star certification in package pricing
Active social media community (Instagram, WeChat, Xiaohongshu) and Ctrip rating around 4.7/5 from 23+ reviews
cons: Limited English support compared to foreigner-oriented schools like Surfway Hainan or Hang5
No dedicated kids' enclosed pool and no formal accessibility program
Wave conditions depend entirely on natural ocean - flat days and crowded lineups in peak winter season
Cancellation policy is strict (>24 h reschedule required, <24 h non-refundable)
Less polished operations and less brand-partnership backing than the new China Tourism Group / SURFLAND ecosystem next door
reviews_reputation.user_reviews: Ctrip rating ~4.7/5 from 23+ reviews praising patient coaches, photography service and the integrated cafe-bar atmosphere; Xiaohongshu and Dianping reviewers describe Surfplus as a 'community-style' school with good vibe and reasonable prices; some criticism around scheduling flexibility and English support [uncertain]
reviews_reputation.recommended_for: Domestic surf travelers (including Mandarin-speaking foreign residents); first-time surfers wanting a relaxed community vibe; couples and small friend groups; those who want surf + beachside cafe / bar in one stop; intermediate surfers progressing to ISA 1-star / 2-star certification
reviews_reputation.operator_brand_partnership: Independent local Wanning surf-community brand; operates in parallel and complementary to the SURFLAND PerfectSwell resort (which opened Nov 2025); part of the broader Riyue Bay 'Tianxin Village / Nanhai Fishing Village' surf cluster of 50+ clubs; not formally affiliated with China Tourism Group or American Wave Machines

basic_info.name: Surfway Hainan Surf School (Surfway Hainan 衝浪學校)
basic_info.location: Inside SURFLAND Wanning Surf Resort, Riyue Bay Tourism Area, Liji Town, Wanning City, Hainan Province, China (海南萬寧·日月灣 SURFLAND 中旅逐浪度假區內)
basic_info.operator: Surfway Hainan - the official in-house surf school of SURFLAND (operated in partnership with the SURFLAND / 中旅逐浪 resort and aligned with American Wave Machines / PerfectSwell coaching standards)

technical_specs.wave_technology_brand: PerfectSwell by American Wave Machines (school exclusively teaches inside the SURFLAND PerfectSwell pool; also offers ocean private classes at adjacent Riyue Bay)
technical_specs.pool_size: Operates in the SURFLAND ~10,000 m² PerfectSwell wave basin (Riyue Bay)
technical_specs.wave_height: 0.3-2.7 m (selected per skill tier; Beginner sessions use the small/whitewater settings, PRO sessions can request 2 m+ barrels)
technical_specs.wave_height_adjustability: Yes - the underlying PerfectSwell pool offers 23 programmable wave settings, and the school books different settings for Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced and PRO tiers
technical_specs.wave_types: Artificial PerfectSwell waves: soft whitewater (Beginner), peeling green-wave shoulders (Intermediate), steep faces with sections (Advanced), barrels and aerial ramps (PRO)
technical_specs.waves_per_hour: Up to ~600 waves/hour at the underlying pool; per-student wave count depends on group size and rotation [uncertain]
technical_specs.wave_count_per_set: 4 waves per set with ~6-second intervals (inherited from the SURFLAND PerfectSwell pool)
technical_specs.session_length: 55 minutes per group session
technical_specs.stance_rotation_schedule: Yes - because SURFLAND alternates Goofy / Regular days, the school requires students to confirm stance and select a date matching their preferred orientation
technical_specs.wave_type_classification: Artificial PerfectSwell - predictable peel similar to a point break; coaching curriculum is built around repeatable take-off zones rather than ocean variability

pricing_sessions.price_range: Group lessons 388-545 RMB per 55-minute session (board / wetsuit rental extra); private bookings 7,760-12,160 RMB
pricing_sessions.session_skill_tiers: Group sessions (55 minutes): Beginner 398 RMB, Intermediate 388 RMB, Advanced 498 RMB, PRO 545 RMB. Private PerfectSwell bookings: Intermediate 7,760 RMB (1 hr) / 8,560 RMB (2 hr); Advanced 9,960 RMB (1 hr) / 10,560 RMB (2 hr); PRO (barrels/aerials) 10,150 RMB (1 hr) / 12,160 RMB (2 hr)
pricing_sessions.equipment_rental: Hardtop surfboard (basic) 80 RMB per session with 500 RMB refundable deposit; Hardtop surfboard (pro) 300 RMB/day; wetsuit 40 RMB with 100 RMB refundable deposit; leash typically included with board rental
pricing_sessions.coaching_fee: Land coaching + in-water session coaching combo 600 RMB; wave-pushing assistance 328 RMB; ocean private class (at natural Riyue Bay) 900 RMB
pricing_sessions.coaching_packages: Complete package (land coaching + in-water coaching + filming + video analysis) 1,175 RMB; video analysis + filming bundle 580 RMB
pricing_sessions.membership_program: No publicly listed annual / monthly / 10-pack card; multi-session and discounted bundles handled on request [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.corporate_team_building: Yes - school accepts group bookings, team-building events and private pool buyouts via the SURFLAND resort; specific minimum group size and pricing handled case by case [uncertain]

experience_guide.best_season: Year-round - the school operates inside the climate-independent SURFLAND wave pool; demand peaks during Chinese national holidays (Oct, Spring Festival, May Day) and summer school break
experience_guide.best_swell_window: N/A - in-pool school; for the optional ocean private class at adjacent Riyue Bay, the natural swell window is October to March
experience_guide.crowd_level: Group sessions can fill quickly during weekends and holidays; private bookings have minimal queues; weekday morning and late-evening sessions tend to be the calmest
experience_guide.booking_method: Direct booking through the school's website surfway-hainan.com, plus messaging via WhatsApp, Telegram or email; also bookable through the SURFLAND resort booking channels (WeChat mini-program, Ctrip, Dianping)
experience_guide.booking_platform: Official site surfway-hainan.com (English-language); WhatsApp; Telegram; Email; SURFLAND WeChat mini-program; Ctrip / Trip.com (携程); Dianping (大眾點評)
experience_guide.payment_options: International bank cards (Visa / Mastercard) accepted - a key differentiator for foreign visitors; Alipay and WeChat Pay also accepted; cash typically not preferred
experience_guide.skill_required: All levels - tiered curriculum from total beginner to PRO; students self-select tier and the school may re-grade after a check-up
experience_guide.age_eligibility: Aligned with SURFLAND venue rules: ages 6-60, minimum height ~1.0 m; under-6 and over-60 generally not admitted to in-water lessons
experience_guide.child_friendly_score: 4 out of 5 - dedicated Beginner tier with soft-tops, English-speaking coordinator, family-friendly hotel options on site; minimum age 6 and 1 m height limit exclude very young children
experience_guide.sup_paddleboard_offering: Not listed as a core school offering; SUP rentals available at the adjacent natural Riyue Bay [uncertain]
experience_guide.photography_video_service: Filming 520 RMB; drone filming (3 batteries) 750 RMB; video analysis + filming bundle 580 RMB; complete coaching + filming + analysis package 1,175 RMB; SURFLAND venue's own 3 complimentary photos plus 199 RMB dynamic video upgrade also available
experience_guide.language_support: Strong English support - the school explicitly markets a 'personal coordinator with fluent English' and is positioned as the foreigner-friendly entry point into SURFLAND; Mandarin Chinese also supported; other languages on request [uncertain]
experience_guide.accessibility_for_disabled: No publicly listed adaptive surfing program or barrier-free in-water lesson at this time [uncertain]
experience_guide.stance_preference_input: Yes - students must declare Goofy or Regular at booking and pick a date matching the SURFLAND pool's daily rotation
experience_guide.safety_notes: Coaches drawn from the Riyue Bay surf community, including ISA-certified instructors and members of the China National Surfing Team coaching pool; mandatory pre-session safety briefing; lifeguards on deck; helmets typically required for Advanced and PRO settings; travel insurance recommended
experience_guide.events_competitions_calendar: School supports student progression toward national-team trials and future ISA / WSL events held at SURFLAND; no public school-run competition calendar yet [uncertain]

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: Surfway Hainan partners with on-resort hotels: Garden-view rooms from 780 RMB and sea-view rooms from 980 RMB (breakfast included). Off-resort options include Sweetome Surf Hotel, Forest Inn Riyue Bay Surf Branch and numerous Riyue Bay surf guesthouses ~300-1,200 RMB/night
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: Located inside SURFLAND - direct access to the resort's two on-site hotels (Yue Chao 越潮 / 樾潮 + Yu Yue 御越 / 屿玥, 132 rooms total). Yue Chao includes a constant-temperature pool, kids' themed rooms, pet-friendly options and a complimentary 1-hour dual surf lesson
surroundings.nearby_food: SURFLAND's X-LIVIN Street (逐浪越界潮流商業街區) inside the resort provides cafés, restaurants, a beachfront club and a live-music venue; surrounding Liji Town / Riyue Bay surf village offers Hainanese seafood, hotpot and beachside surf cafés
surroundings.transport: Reachable by self-drive, Didi ride-hailing, taxi or resort shuttle; private car charters from Sanya and Haikou are common
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: ~70 minutes by car from Sanya Phoenix International Airport (SYX); ~18.4 km / ~20-minute drive from Shenzhou High-Speed Railway Station; ~2 hours from Haikou Meilan International Airport (HAK)
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Half-day Surfway lesson at SURFLAND wave pool + half-day natural ocean session at Riyue Bay + Xinglong Tropical Botanical Garden + Shenzhou Peninsula day trip + cultural sites in Wanning; multi-day learn-to-surf packages pair AM pool session with PM ocean coaching

reviews_reputation.pros: 1) Direct access to the only PerfectSwell pool in China with consistent waves at every skill tier; 2) Tiered pricing keeps Beginner and Intermediate group sessions under 500 RMB; 3) English-speaking coordinator and acceptance of international bank cards make it the most foreigner-friendly school in Hainan; 4) Strong filming and video-analysis add-ons for skill progression; 5) Coaches drawn from Riyue Bay's deep ISA-certified instructor pool
reviews_reputation.cons: 1) Private session pricing is very high (up to 12,160 RMB) compared to ocean lessons in the same bay; 2) Tied to SURFLAND's strict 6-60 age band and 1 m height minimum; 3) Booking subject to the venue's daily Goofy/Regular rotation, which can be inconvenient for travelers on short trips; 4) Equipment rental and filming are charged separately on top of session price; 5) New school (post-Nov 2025), so long-term review base is still small
reviews_reputation.user_reviews: Limited consolidated review base as of mid-2026 (school opened with the resort on 2025-11-11); early traveler accounts on Xiaohongshu and surf blogs praise the English support, coaching quality and PerfectSwell wave consistency, while criticizing the steep private pricing and queue management on holidays [uncertain]
reviews_reputation.recommended_for: International visitors seeking English-speaking instruction in China; intermediate-to-advanced surfers who want repeatable, high-quality waves to drill maneuvers; PRO surfers preparing for barrels/aerials; families with kids 6+; corporate team-building groups using the SURFLAND venue
reviews_reputation.operator_brand_partnership: Surfway Hainan operates as the official in-house surf school of SURFLAND (中旅逐浪度假區), itself a partnership between China Tourism Group (中旅集團) and American Wave Machines (PerfectSwell technology); coaching staff overlap with the Riyue Bay national-team and ISA-certified instructor community

verification.verification_sources: 1) Surfway Hainan official site surfway-hainan.com (pricing, payment methods, English coordinator, on-resort lodging rates); 2) China Daily Hainan article on SURFLAND opening 2025-11-11; 3) WavePool Magazine PerfectSwell Hainan testing report; 4) Tropical Hainan article on Olympic-standard wave pool opening; 5) Vast Surf Shop SURFLAND review blog; 6) Hotel News Resource and Korea Herald wire releases on the resort opening; 7) Ctrip listing for 万宁日月湾中旅逐浪樾潮酒店 (hotel ID 132247344); 8) ISA International Surfing Association article on Italian surf explorer kickstarting Chinese surfing (ISA Level-2 coach pool at Riyue Bay)

basic_info.name: Wenchang Tonggu Ridge Artificial Wave Pool (文昌·銅鼓嶺人工浪池) - NOT VERIFIED. No artificial wave pool exists or is under construction at Tonggu Ridge as of 2026-05. Tonggu Ridge (Tongguling 銅鼓嶺) is a National AAAA-level natural mountain-and-sea scenic area; the adjacent Moon Bay (月亮灣) and Qishui Bay (淇水灣) are NATURAL beach breaks - sometimes referred to in tourism marketing as 'Hainan's largest natural surfing destination', but with no man-made wave pool.
basic_info.location: Longlou Town, Wenchang City, Hainan (海南省文昌市龍樓鎮·銅鼓嶺景區). The wider Tonggu Ridge International Eco-Tourism Zone covers 86 km² (46 km² land + 40 km² sea), encompassing Moon Bay, Qishui Bay, Stone Park, etc., approximately 30 km from downtown Wenchang. No specific wave-pool address exists.

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: Sea-view B&Bs in Longlou Town (e.g., Guanlange Sea-view Inn at Qishui Bay, popular for rocket-launch viewing). Resort options are limited compared to Sanya/Wanning. Price range roughly 300-1500 RMB per night.
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: [uncertain] - No on-site accommodation tied to an artificial wave pool because none exists.
surroundings.nearby_food: Local Wenchang specialty restaurants featuring Wenchang chicken, fresh seafood, and Hainanese coastal cuisine. Casual dining clusters around Longlou Town and Qishui Bay. Limited Western options.
surroundings.transport: Self-drive recommended; taxi or didi (网约车) available. Bus options from Wenchang downtown to Longlou Town. Hainan Coastal Highway (G98 island ring) provides easy access.
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: Approximately 70-90 km (~90-120 minutes) from Haikou Meilan International Airport (HAK). Wenchang High-speed Rail Station is about 30-40 km away (~40-50 minutes by car). The Wenchang Commercial Spaceport is in close proximity to Tonggu Ridge.
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Tonggu Ridge + Moon Bay + Qishui Bay + Stone Park + Wenchang Spaceport (rocket launches) + Wennan Old Street + Spring Light Coconut Kingdom (春光椰子王國) form the standard Wenchang east-coast tourism circuit. No wave pool can be added to this itinerary.

Conclusion: no artificial wave pool exists or is in construction at Tonggu Ridge as of 2026-05. The area remains a natural mountain-sea scenic destination.

verification.verification_sources: Verification searches conducted 2026-05-15 returned no evidence that a wave pool branded 'Vigour Coast OUTLETS' or '活力海岸 OUTLETS 浪池' exists in Haikou. Sources consulted: (1) WavePoolMag 'New Wave Pools 2026: All the Surf Parks Opening This Year' lists every wave pool opening worldwide in 2026 - no Chinese facility appears: https://wavepoolmag.com/new-wave-pools-2026/ ; (2) Wavegarden's official 'Six new world-class surf destinations on the horizon' announcement (April 2026) names DSRT Surf USA, El Nido Uruguay, Surfers Cove Portugal, Gemswell Surf Madrid Spain, Perth Surf Park Australia, Auckland Surf Park NZ - no Hainan or Haikou project: https://amusementtoday.com/2026/04/6-new-wavegarden-world-class-surf-destinations-on-the-horizon/ ; (3) CR MixC Haikou (华润万象城) Baidu Baike confirms address 4 Jinmao East Road, Longhua District, opened 2022-06-26, 12.3万㎡, pure island-tourism-themed shopping mall with no wave-pool component: https://baike.baidu.com/item/万象城/61621726 ; (4) Tencent News on Haikou West Coast 1.92万亩 master plan (2025-05-27) describes a 12.8 km² mixed-use coastal redevelopment using the '活力海岸' / 'Vibrant Coast' brand concept, but no Wavegarden Cove or named wave-pool tenant is listed: https://news.qq.com/rain/a/20250527A07JSF00 ; (5) Local-news sweep across Sohu, Hainan Daily, Haikou Bendibao, Hainan.gov.cn, Sina Hainan, CGTN, TropicalHainan, Surfer, Surfline confirms the only operating Olympic-grade artificial wave pool in Hainan as of 2026-05 is SURFLAND PerfectSwell at Wanning Riyue Bay (~60 km from Haikou), not in Haikou: https://www.tropicalhainan.com/chinas-first-olympic-standard-wave-pool-opens-in-wanning-hainan/ , https://www.koreaherald.com/article/10623347 ; (6) The most prominent new Haikou waterfront commercial project '浪潮里' on Haidian Island (Sina Finance 2026-04-29) is a 30,000㎡ riverside lifestyle complex with no wave-/surf-functionality despite the 'wave' wordplay in its name: https://finance.sina.com.cn/jjxw/2026-04-29/doc-inhwckrq6798021.shtml ; (7) Haikou Bendibao 2026 events index lists no wave-pool opening or surfing event in Haikou: https://m.haikou.bendibao.com/xiuxian/huodongchaxun/ ; (8) Xiaohongshu / Zhihu user-generated content on Haikou surfing only references natural beach surfing at locations such as Holiday Beach (假日海滩), Xiying Beach (西秀海滩), and the Hou Hai natural surf village (in Sanya, not Haikou). Conclusion: as of 2026-05-15 the entry 'Haikou Vigour Coast OUTLETS Wave Pool' is most likely a misattribution or speculative listing; no operating, under-construction, or formally announced wave pool by that name has been identified.

basic_info.name: Wanning Surf Festival / National Surfing Championship (万宁国际冲浪赛 / 全国冲浪锦标赛) - umbrella term covering the annual cluster of surf competitions held at Riyue Bay (Sun & Moon Bay), including the National Surfing Championship, China (Hainan) Surfing Open, Hainan Provincial Surfing Championship, ISA China Cup (historical), and WSL International Wanning Corona Cero Pro (returned 2025)
basic_info.location: Riyue Bay (日月湾) National Surf Training Base, Wanning City, Hainan Province, China (between Wanning and Lingshui counties, ~18°N latitude)
basic_info.operator: Sanctioned by the State General Administration of Sport's Water Sports Management Center (国家体育总局水上运动管理中心) and Chinese Surfing Association; co-organized with Hainan Provincial Sports Bureau and Wanning Municipal Government; international events sanctioned by World Surf League (WSL) and International Surfing Association (ISA)
basic_info.opening_date: N/A (annual event series; National Surfing Championship founded 2017; Wanning International Surfing Festival/Open in operation since ~2010 - 14th edition held in 2024)

technical_specs.wave_technology_brand: N/A (natural surf - left point break and beach break at Riyue Bay); note 2026 also added an artificial wave-pool national event (全国人工冲浪池公开赛) hosted at the new Riyue Bay Surf Resort/中旅逐浪度假区 in March 2026
technical_specs.pool_size: not_applicable (natural surf venue; competition area set up along Riyue Bay shoreline)
technical_specs.wave_height: Typical competition-season wave height ~0.6-2.0 m; winter swell window (Oct-Mar) regularly delivers head-high to overhead sets at the left point [uncertain - exact contest-day heights vary]
technical_specs.wave_height_adjustability: not_applicable (natural ocean swell)
technical_specs.wave_types: Long peeling left-hand point break (primary contest peak) plus beach-break peaks; suitable for shortboard, longboard and junior divisions
technical_specs.waves_per_hour: not_applicable
technical_specs.wave_count_per_set: not_applicable (natural ocean sets)
technical_specs.session_length: Heat formats follow WSL/ISA/CSA standard (typically 20-30 min heats); full event waiting periods run 7-8 days per competition
technical_specs.stance_rotation_schedule: not_applicable
technical_specs.wave_type_classification: Natural left-hand point break - same latitude (~18°N) as Australia's Gold Coast; rated among the world's top 10 surf destinations and considered China's premier natural surf spot

pricing_sessions.price_range: Spectator access generally free; competitor entry fees set per event by sanctioning body (CSA/WSL/ISA) [uncertain - exact figures not published publicly]
pricing_sessions.session_skill_tiers: National Surfing Championship divisions: Open Group, U18 Group, U15 Group across Men's Shortboard, Women's Shortboard, Men's Longboard, Women's Longboard. WSL Wanning Corona Cero Pro: Men's QS 4000, Women's QS 4000, Men's Pro Junior, Women's Pro Junior. ISA China Cup (historical): national-team format with shortboard men/women; Hainan Classic legacy 4-star ASP/QS event
pricing_sessions.equipment_rental: not_applicable (athletes bring their own boards; Riyue Bay village has 160+ surf clubs offering rental for visiting public)
pricing_sessions.coaching_fee: not_applicable
pricing_sessions.coaching_packages: not_applicable
pricing_sessions.membership_program: not_applicable
pricing_sessions.corporate_team_building: not_applicable (event series; however Wanning markets a year-round '一赛多游 / one-event multi-tour' model bundling competition viewing with tourism packages)

experience_guide.best_season: October through March is the prime competition window aligned with the NE monsoon swell. Key calendar anchors observed in 2024-2026: late March-early April (China/Hainan Surfing Open), October (Hainan Provincial Surfing Championship), early-mid December (Wanning International Surfing Open / WSL Wanning Corona Cero Pro), late December (National Surfing Championship). 2026 also added an artificial-pool national event in early March
experience_guide.best_swell_window: October to March - aligned with NE monsoon delivering long-period swell to the left point; December is the peak competition month
experience_guide.crowd_level: Heavy crowds during competition weeks - hotel rates spike, line-ups in non-contest peaks remain crowded; Wanning recorded 6.67 million tourist visits in first 8 months of 2025 with ~500,000 annual surfing-experience visitors
experience_guide.booking_method: Athlete entry through Chinese Surfing Association (CSA) for national events, World Surf League athlete portal for WSL QS/Pro Junior events, ISA national federation entry for ISA-sanctioned events; spectator viewing on-site free
experience_guide.booking_platform: WSL: worldsurfleague.com event page; CSA national events: Chinese Surfing Association official channels; local schedule announcements via Wanning Municipal Government website (wanning.hainan.gov.cn) and Hainan Provincial Sports Bureau
experience_guide.payment_options: not_applicable (spectator); athlete entry fees handled via federation/WSL portals
experience_guide.skill_required: Competitor: Advanced/Pro (selection through provincial/national qualifying); Spectator: none
experience_guide.age_eligibility: U15 (under 15), U18 (under 18), Open (adult) divisions for National Championship; Pro Junior (under 18) and Open QS divisions for WSL events
experience_guide.child_friendly_score: 3 - family-friendly viewing on the beach; concurrent surf-village activities, music festivals and beach markets cater to children, but actual competition spectating requires sun/heat tolerance
experience_guide.sup_paddleboard_offering: Riyue Bay has historically hosted ISA World StandUp Paddle Championships in addition to surfing events [uncertain - SUP not part of every annual edition]
experience_guide.photography_video_service: WSL events live-streamed on worldsurfleague.com; national events broadcast/streamed via CCTV-5 and provincial channels [uncertain - per-event specifics vary]
experience_guide.language_support: WSL events: English + Mandarin; national events: Mandarin primary
experience_guide.accessibility_for_disabled: [uncertain] - no public information on adaptive surfing divisions at the Wanning festival
experience_guide.stance_preference_input: not_applicable
experience_guide.safety_notes: Sanctioned events use ISA-certified judges and on-water safety personnel (water-patrol skis, lifeguards); competition zones are coned off and recreational surfers excluded during heats
experience_guide.events_competitions_calendar: ANNUAL FESTIVAL CALENDAR (Riyue Bay / National Surf Training Base): (1) March-April: 中国(海南)冲浪公开赛 China (Hainan) Surfing Open - 2025 edition Mar 28-Apr 2 with 39 teams/300+ athletes; (2) March 2026 NEW: 全国人工冲浪池公开赛 National Artificial Wave Pool Open Mar 7-9, 2026 at adjacent CTG Wave Resort; (3) October: 海南省冲浪锦标赛 Hainan Provincial Surfing Championship (2024 edition concluded Oct 15); (4) Early-Mid December: 万宁国际冲浪赛 Wanning International Surfing Open / WSL International Wanning Corona Cero Pro QS 4000 + Pro Junior - 2024 edition (14th) Dec 8-15, 2025 edition Dec 11-17 (first WSL event in China since Jan 2020, prize purse not officially disclosed); (5) Late December: 全国冲浪锦标赛 National Surfing Championship - 2020 Dec 21-29, 2023 Dec 5-11, 2024 Dec 27, 2025 Dec 20-27 (36 teams, ~300 athletes). Historical: ISA China Cup + Hainan Classic ran 2014 in November. Wanning hosts ~42 sport-tourism events annually under '潮玩山海·浪动万宁' theme. PRACTICAL IMPACT: expect partial beach/lineup closures (封场) during competition heats roughly 3-4 weeks per year clustered Mar-Apr, Oct, and especially Dec; book accommodation 1-2 months ahead for Dec window

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: Riyue Bay surf village hosts 130+ guesthouses/B&Bs; on-site Riyue Bay Surf Resort (中旅逐浪度假区, opened Nov 11, 2025) provides flagship contest hotel; rates spike sharply during contest weeks
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: Riyue Bay Surf Resort (CTG Group / 中旅集团 逐浪度假区) - flagship surf-themed resort opened Nov 11, 2025 to anchor 2026 Hainan Marine Tourism Year [uncertain - exact room count and rates]
surroundings.nearby_food: Surf-village cafes and seafood restaurants in Riyue Bay; 160+ surf clubs many with attached F&B; hotel restaurants at Shimei Bay (石梅湾) and Shenzhou Peninsula (神州半岛) within 20-40 min drive
surroundings.transport: Self-drive via G98 Hainan Ring Expressway; new Riyue Bay interchange (日月湾互通) opened to improve access; high-speed-rail to Shenzhou or Wanning station then taxi/网约车 (~30-40 min)
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: Sanya Phoenix International Airport (SYX) ~90-100 km / ~75-90 min by car; Haikou Meilan International Airport (HAK) ~200 km / ~2.5 h via expressway/HSR
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Riyue Bay (contest viewing + surf village) + Shimei Bay (Nine-Mile Cultural Bay - hosts concurrent surf-music-festival events) + Shenzhou Peninsula (golf, marine sports park planned) + Xinglong Tropical Botanical Garden + Wanning city; '一赛多游' (one-event multi-tour) packages bundle competition + tourism

pros: Only national-level surf training base in China - hosts both grassroots qualifying and elite WSL/ISA events under one umbrella
Reliable winter swell window (Oct-Mar) gives consistent contestable conditions
December cluster (WSL Pro + National Championship) creates a global-attention moment for Chinese surfing
Strong government and CTG Group backing - 2025 saw return of WSL after 5-year hiatus and opening of flagship surf resort
Local surf-village ecosystem (160+ clubs, 130+ B&Bs) supports family-friendly festival atmosphere
cons: Competition weeks lock up the prime left-point lineup - recreational surfers must shift to secondary peaks
Accommodation prices and crowding spike sharply during contest windows (especially mid-late December)
Date confirmation often comes <8 weeks out, complicating travel planning
Wave conditions ocean-dependent - some heats run in marginal surf
Festival schedule fragmented across multiple sanctioning bodies (CSA/ISA/WSL/Hainan Province) - no single consolidated calendar
reviews_reputation.user_reviews: [uncertain] - no aggregated review score available; coverage is via sports media (Xinhua, China News Service, CCTV-5) and WSL editorial; local Xiaohongshu/Mafengwo notes describe heavy crowds but festive atmosphere during December competition window
reviews_reputation.recommended_for: Advanced surfers and competitors (athlete entry); surf-tourism spectators and photographers (December peak); families combining surf-watch with Hainan resort vacation; sports-tourism researchers and industry attendees
reviews_reputation.operator_brand_partnership: Chinese Surfing Association × State Sport General Administration Water Sports Management Center; WSL Asia Region partnership with Wanning Municipal Government; CTG Group (中国旅游集团 / China Tourism Group) operating Riyue Bay Surf Resort as official venue partner; title sponsorship of WSL event by Corona Cero (AB InBev)
