basic_info.name: Wet'n'Wild Haikou (海口·观澜湖狂野水世界), also marketed locally as 海口狂欢水世界
basic_info.location: Mission Hills Centreville Haikou, No. 63 Yangshan Avenue, Longhua District, Haikou, Hainan Province, China
basic_info.operator: Joint venture between Village Roadshow Theme Parks (VRTP, Australia), Guangxi Investment Group, and Mission Hills Group
basic_info.opening_date: 2018-05-01 (soft-opened end of April 2018; grand opening early May 2018)

technical_specs.wave_technology_brand: WhiteWater West (large family wave pool) and a separate FlowRider/Surf Machine stationary wave attraction (Surf Rider) [uncertain on exact FlowRider model]
technical_specs.pool_size: 3,700 m² wave pool (Wild Water Bay) within a total park footprint of approximately 50,000 m²; project also includes an 8,000 m² indoor waterpark area
technical_specs.wave_height: Family wave pool produces variable shaped waves (approx. 0.3-1.2 m); Surf Rider sheet-wave stationary attraction generates a continuous standing wave [uncertain on exact peak heights]
technical_specs.wave_height_adjustability: Yes - the wave pool system can produce different wave shapes and sizes through programmable cycles; specific tier names/heights not publicly published [uncertain]
technical_specs.wave_types: Recreational rolling/white-water waves in the main wave pool (Wild Water Bay) suitable for bobbing, bodyboarding and floating; standing sheet-wave on the Surf Rider attraction allowing flowboarding and bodyboarding (not traditional surfing)
technical_specs.waves_per_hour: [uncertain] - operates in scheduled cycles with rest intervals throughout the day; not publicly documented
technical_specs.wave_count_per_set: [uncertain] - operates in cycles between wave modes; specific set count not published
technical_specs.session_length: Wave pool: continuous access during park hours with cycled sets. Surf Rider: typically short individual rides (~30-60 seconds per turn) under lifeguard rotation [uncertain]
technical_specs.stance_rotation_schedule: N/A - not a surf training facility; Surf Rider is a stationary wave with no Goofy/Regular daily rotation
technical_specs.wave_type_classification: Artificial recreational wave pool (family wave pool) plus a stationary sheet-wave (Surf Rider/FlowRider-type) - not a high-performance surfing wave pool

pricing_sessions.price_range: Standard adult day ticket approximately RMB 238-280 (varies by season/platform); child and senior tickets discounted; combined Mission Hills Centreville passes (waterpark + Wonder Adventure indoor park) available [uncertain on exact current pricing - park has had seasonal closures]
pricing_sessions.session_skill_tiers: N/A - no skill-tiered surf sessions; the Surf Rider is open-access on a queue basis included with park admission
pricing_sessions.equipment_rental: Life jackets included free with admission; bodyboards/flowboards provided at the Surf Rider; locker rental available [uncertain on exact fees]
pricing_sessions.coaching_fee: N/A - no formal surf coaching program at the venue; lifeguards provide basic Surf Rider technique guidance
pricing_sessions.coaching_packages: N/A
pricing_sessions.membership_program: Mission Hills resort membership and annual passes available through Mission Hills Centreville; specific Wet'n'Wild-only membership pricing not publicly listed [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.corporate_team_building: Yes - Mission Hills Haikou actively markets MICE, team-building, and corporate event packages including Wet'n'Wild buyouts and group bookings

experience_guide.best_season: April-October when air/water temperatures are warmest in Haikou; park traditionally closes during winter months and after typhoon damage. Note: park experienced extended closure starting September 2024 due to typhoon damage - confirm reopening status before visit [uncertain]
experience_guide.best_swell_window: N/A - artificial wave pool
experience_guide.crowd_level: High during Chinese national holidays (Golden Week, Spring Festival) and summer school holidays; moderate on weekends; quieter on weekdays in shoulder season
experience_guide.booking_method: Online pre-purchase recommended through Chinese OTA platforms; on-site ticket purchase available
experience_guide.booking_platform: Meituan/Dianping (大众点评), Ctrip (携程), Fliggy (飞猪), Tuniu (途牛), Qunar (去哪儿), Mission Hills Centreville WeChat Mini Program; international visitors can use Klook/Trip.com when listed
experience_guide.payment_options: Alipay, WeChat Pay, UnionPay, cash (RMB); international credit cards accepted at on-site box office and Mission Hills resort facilities
experience_guide.skill_required: Zero for the wave pool; basic balance recommended for the Surf Rider stationary wave attraction
experience_guide.age_eligibility: Standard waterpark restrictions apply: minimum height requirements on thrill slides (typically 1.2 m+); children under 1.2 m must be accompanied by adult; specific Surf Rider age/weight limits typically 6+ years and 25 kg minimum [uncertain on exact thresholds]
experience_guide.child_friendly_score: 5 - highly child-friendly with dedicated Wet'n'Wild Junior children's zone, family wave pool with shallow zero-entry edges, lifeguards posted, free life jackets
experience_guide.sup_paddleboard_offering: Not offered within the waterpark [uncertain]
experience_guide.photography_video_service: On-ride photo capture at major slides; photo packages sold at exit kiosks; pricing varies [uncertain on exact rates]
experience_guide.language_support: Limited English signage; some English-speaking front-desk staff at Mission Hills Centreville given Australian VRTP operator partnership; primary operating language is Mandarin Chinese
experience_guide.accessibility_for_disabled: Standard waterpark accessibility - wheelchair accessible pathways for general areas and family wave pool entry; no dedicated adaptive surfing program; specific accessibility provisions for Surf Rider not publicly documented [uncertain]
experience_guide.stance_preference_input: N/A - not a surf training pool
experience_guide.safety_notes: Operated to international VRTP safety standards; lifeguards stationed at all pools and slides; mandatory life jackets for non-swimmers/children; wave cycle warnings before sets; insurance included per Chinese theme park regulations; pregnant women and those with heart conditions advised against the wave pool and Surf Rider
experience_guide.events_competitions_calendar: Daily live entertainment and DJ shows on the wave pool's large LED screen; seasonal themed events (summer water carnival, holiday specials); does not host competitive surfing events

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: Mission Hills Resort Haikou (5-star, on-site at the larger resort complex), Hard Rock Hotel Haikou (within Mission Hills Centreville), Renaissance Haikou Hotel - typical rates RMB 800-2,500+ per night
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: Mission Hills Resort Haikou and Hard Rock Hotel Haikou are part of the same Mission Hills Centreville complex; package deals bundling Wet'n'Wild access with hotel stays are commonly offered
surroundings.nearby_food: Mission Hills Centreville features extensive dining (China's largest single-development F&B precinct) including Hainan local cuisine, international restaurants, cafes, and bars; on-site Wet'n'Wild park has F&B outlets and quick-service dining
surroundings.transport: Self-drive (large parking lot available), taxi/Didi from Haikou city center (approximately 25-40 minutes), Mission Hills shuttle bus from Haikou Meilan International Airport and Haikou city
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: Approximately 25-30 km / 30-40 minutes drive from Haikou Meilan International Airport (HAK); approximately 20 km from Haikou Railway Station; complimentary shuttle service typically provided by Mission Hills resort for hotel guests
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Combine with Mission Hills Haikou volcanic hot springs (216 mineral/spring pools), Mission Hills golf courses (10 courses), Hainan Movie Town (Feng Xiaogang Movie Commune), Wonder Adventure indoor theme park, and Haikou Volcanic Cluster Geopark

pros: First international-brand water park in China with strong VRTP operational pedigree
3,700 m² wave pool with large LED entertainment screen for live shows
Iconic 360Rush vertical loop slide and other thrill rides not found elsewhere in Hainan
Integrated within Mission Hills Centreville for easy combination with hot springs, golf, and dining
Strong safety standards from Australian operator partnership
cons: Park has had extended closure periods (e.g., post-typhoon Sep 2024); operating status volatile - always verify before visit
Seasonal operation (typically closed in winter); not year-round like Atlantis Sanya
Surf Rider attraction is recreational, not a surf training environment
Long queues during Chinese national holidays and summer break
Limited English-language guest services compared to international standards
reviews_reputation.user_reviews: Dianping (大众点评) average rating approximately 4.0-4.5/5 historically; Xiaohongshu and Mafengwo coverage praises the slide variety and 360Rush; mixed reviews on operating reliability and seasonal closures; some recent posts note typhoon damage closure 2024-2025 [uncertain on exact current scores]
reviews_reputation.recommended_for: Families with children, thrill-seeking teenagers, Mission Hills resort guests, group/team-building bookings, day-trippers from Haikou; NOT recommended for serious surfers seeking training waves
reviews_reputation.operator_brand_partnership: Village Roadshow Theme Parks (VRTP, Australia - operator of original Wet'n'Wild Gold Coast and Sydney parks) × Guangxi Investment Group × Mission Hills Group joint venture; design by Water Technology Inc. (WTI, USA); attractions supplied by WhiteWater West Industries (Canada) and ProSlide; first international waterpark brand in China
